ChargeGuru works with charging station manufacturers, installers, and car makers to offer a seamless customer journey to all who decided to switch to clean mobility. 

ChargeGuru is the single point of contact for customers to get the optimal charging solutions and have them installed by electricians experienced in this field. ChargeGuru can deliver the complete package: Installation, maintenance, monitoring, administration, management plus integration of regional subsidies plus all administrative processes. By operating a network of electricians and verifying their expertise in installing charging infrastructure, ChargeGuru can quickly hire the correct local electrician to install the charging infrastructure. ChargeGuru has a countrywide coverage in its operating countries.
tags: Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / EV Charging / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Renewable Energy / Smart Buildings / Smart Home / Sustainability / Transportation

name: ChargeLab
one_liner: ChargeLab is the operating system for EV charging.
description:
ChargeLab builds software to power smart electric vehicle charging. It makes it easy for EV drivers to fill up, easy for building owners to deploy smarter infrastructure, and easy for service providers to offer turnkey EV charging solutions.

ChargeLab is a preferred software partner of leading EV charger manufacturers including ABB, Delta Electronics, Phihong, and Siemens.
tags: API / Distributed Energy / Energy Optimization / EV / Electronic Vehicles / EV Charging / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Smart Buildings / Smart Home / Smart Charging / Smart City / Smart Infrastructure / Software as a Service / SaaS

name: HySiLabs
one_liner: HySiLabs has developed an emission-free liquid hydrogen-carrier enabling to release hydrogen on-demand, with no energy input required. The liquid hydrogen-carrier is stable, non-toxic, and non-explosive, enabling the same logistics as conventional fuels.
description: Supply chain logistics software/platform for transportation, warehousing, or fulfillment operations.
investors: EASME - EU Executive Agency for SMEs
tags: B2B / Chemicals / Cleantech / Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Fuel Cells / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Logistics / Maritime / Rail / Renewable Energy / Smart Infrastructure / Sustainability / Transportation

name: PowerUp Energy Technologies
one_liner: Produces portable hydrogen fuel cell generators for maritime and automotive fleet industries
description: PowerUp Energy Technologies solves the problem of providing clean energy with portability by delivering a fuel cell backup generator solution. Their customers include the maritime and automotive fleet industries, who desire portable power packs without the need to rely on heavy automotive batteries or the grid. They provide a range of Fuel cells including the UP200, UP400, UP1000 [planned] and UP2000 [planned], differing by the amount of power outputted.
tags: B2B2C / Battery / Battery Materials / Cleantech / Energy Storage / EV Charging / Fuel Cells / Hardware

name: KRAFTBLOCK
one_liner: Develops modular high-temperature thermal energy storage systems to decarbonize industrial heating processes
description:
KRAFTBLOCK is an energy storage system that is able to decarbonize industries like steel, glass or ceramics. It will even help renewables to have their break-through in industrial heating.

The modular and easily scalable system is based on a newly developed storage material that consists of app. 85% upcycled materials. It can store up to 1.2 MWh thermal energy at a temperature level up to 1,300 °C/2,500°F.

Due to its modular character, a single module might also be used as a mobile storage unit, that enables you to charge the storage in one place, transport it by truck or train to another place and utilize the heat at the new location.

Kraftblock decarbonizes by storing and utilizing formerly lost or wasted heat. This means Kraftblock minimizes the usage of fossil fuels, avoids the formation of carbon dioxide and increases the economic value of formerly lost or wasted energy.
tags: Advanced Materials / Cleantech / Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Renewable Energy / Sustainability

name: Airthium
one_liner: Develops high-efficiency molten salt thermodynamic energy storage systems for grid-scale renewable energy storage and backup power
description:
Airthium builds a new kind of energy storage system, that stores electricity as heat- reversibly.

The system relies on a high temperature heat pump/heat engine to heat molten salt to 540°C ( 1000°F), and then convert the heat back into electricity, with a 70% round trip efficiency, at a typical cost of 110$/kWh without installation. Airthium's units can be used to store and deliver solar and wind energy, starting at 30 kW (small office or workshop) and up to GW scale (Utility).

Moreover, a diesel/biomass burner can be added to the system, to heat up the molten salt after several days of bad weather (no sun/wind), this allows the system to provide uninterruptible power or to go off-grid.
tags: Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Renewable Energy / Sustainability

name: Hydrogenious Technologies
one_liner: Hydrogenious Technologies develops innovative solutions for safe and efficient hydrogen storage in liquid organic hydrogen carriers.
description:
The need for safe and efficient hydrogen storage for multi-MWh energy storage systems and safe and reliable hydrogen storage for hydrogen refuelling stations and fuel cell mobility was imminent – and Liquid Organic Hydrogen Carriers (LOHC) proved to be the solution. Having completed the technological proof of concept at the university’s laboratories, the idea became reality and Hydrogenious Technologies, a global pioneer in LOHC based energy storage, was born.

The LOHC systems enable high-capacity hydrogen handling in the existing fuel infrastructure – reliable, economical and with a small footprint. It can also link the sites where green hydrogen is most efficiently produced with the sites where it is consumed –flexible, simple and at low cost.

Applications include refueling stations, industrial hydrogen and sectorial integration.
investors: Federal Ministry for Economic Affairs and Energy (BMWi)
tags: Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Infrastructure / IT Infrastructure / IT / Renewable Energy / Smart Infrastructure

name: Nostromo Energy
one_liner: Develops ice-based thermal energy storage systems for commercial cooling
description:
Nostromo Energy has developed the most cost-effective, safe and clean Energy storage system available, based on water!
Nostromo IceBrick™, an Ice-based Thermal Energy Storage system, stores electric power during off-peak hours/surplus solar and later uses it for cooling during peak hours. Nostromo's technology can shift up to 95% of the electricity demand used for cooling of commercial customers such as data centers, office buildings, airports, hotels, manufacturing facilities, shopping malls or any other facility that uses a centralized chiller system for cooling. IceBrick™ is energy-efficient, inexpensive and modular. 

Nostromo is based in Israel and is lead by a team of energy industry veterans.
tags: Distributed Energy / Energy Storage / Renewable Energy / Smart Buildings / Smart Home

name: Enapter
one_liner: Manufactures modular anion exchange membrane (AEM) electrolysers for scalable green hydrogen production
description:
Their AEM electrolyser is a completely modular and stackable system to produce hydrogen. Each unit has can work independent from the other modules and produces 500 NL/hr of hydrogen with 99.9% purity. With an optional dryer module 99.999% purity can be achieved. Perfect for small and medium sized hydrogen solutions, the electrolyser can easily be integrated into racks, cabinets or containers. 

The AEM Multicore is their answer to megawatt electrolysers. It features several hundred AEM cores around a balance of plant with built-in redundancy providing the cheapest possible hydrogen even for very large solutions.

Software is an integral part of their offering and the best way to control their electrolysers is via the Enapter energy management system. Enapter’s unique Energy Management System (EMS) allows for intuitive monitoring and control of the electrolysers and dryers, as well as easy integration with third-party devices (ie. fuel cells, sensors, tanks, solar, wind, etc.). 

Hydrogen can be used in a versatile and flexible way, as energy storage, as fuel in vehicles, as raw material in industry or as fuel for heating, especially in areas and regions where direct electrification is difficult to realize. Their goal is to make green hydrogen cheaper than fossil fuels.
tags: Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Oil & Gas / Renewable Energy

name: Malta
one_liner: Malta is developing a high-temperature heat-pump electricity storage system for large-scale long-duration energy storage.
description: Malta’s technology is based on a high-temperature heat-pump electricity storage system for large-scale long-duration energy storage. This technology is well-suited to the changing energy landscape with the potential for system sizes up to several 100 MWs for a single energy storage system, discharge duration capabilities of hours to days, and a plant lifespan of more than 30 years without performance degradation. The system is manufactured from commercially available commodity materials such as steel and salt, adding to its lifetime sustainability attractiveness. Such electricity storage systems are key to the global energy transition underway today, as they can adapt variable resources to the demand curves of consumers to ensure the highest availability and reliability needed at competitive costs.

name: Alumina Energy
one_liner: Develops energy storage systems using recycled ceramics to convert intermittent renewable energy into dispatchable, low-cost power for distributed and utility applications.
description:
Founded in 2015, Alumina Energy is a US developer of energy storage systems for distributed & utility energy generation systems. Using a recycled ceramic materials, their products captures intermittent energy and converts it into a dispatchable, low-cost (levelized energy cost <$80/MWh), zero emissions energy resource.  Due to growing demand, changing consumption and increasing intermittency due to renewable generation, industries need energy storage to help balance the grid and store energy so that it can be dispatchable, per demand. Their product provides a solution to this challenge at all levels of electric and heat generation; 
- supporting peak shaving/demand reduction, integrating intermittent renewable generation, reducing onsite fuel consumption, reduce greenhouse gas emissions, and acting as a source of backup power and heat to end users.

name: EnergyNest
one_liner: Develops modular, scalable thermal energy storage systems using recyclable geomaterials for renewable and industrial energy applications
description:
EnergyNest offers a truly game changing technology for storing thermal energy on a large scale.

EnergyNest delivers a truly unique product that is perfectly tuned towards solving challenges of intermittency in renewable energy and other methods of power generation - providing real, quantifiable value additions to their customers and to society. They help customers across the entire energy system to maximize the value of their energy – from renewable and conventional power producers to industrial energy consumers.

EnergyNest's Thermal Battery technology is modular, scalable and built for purpose. It is made from abundant, recyclable and non-hazardous geomaterials: steel and concrete – HEATCRETE®. Easy to acquire, this means that local workforces and materials can be used, and transportation can be kept to a minimum.

The technology behind EnergyNest's Thermal Battery System is field-proven with verifiable performance. The unique design enables EnergyNest to construct very compact energy storage systems with high energy density, small footprint, and very low energy losses while capacities may range from MWhth to GWhth.
tags: Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Renewable Energy / Sustainability

name: Moment Energy
one_liner: Repurposes retired electric vehicle batteries into energy storage systems for commercial, industrial, and utility applications
description: Electric Vehicle batteries still have up to 80% of their capacity left when they are retired from electric cars. Moment is re-purposing EV batteries to create sustainable energy storage. Their units can be paired with any energy source and will be used to replace diesel generators in remote communities. By re-purposing electric vehicle batteries, they extend their lifetime value and provide a viable option for remote communities to transition from using diesel power. Additionally, Moment Energy's AI-based battery management system (BMS) is a learning system that optimizes the performance of second-life batteries as well as its ability to test, grade and work with batteries from any auto manufacturer therefore not being supply constrained by any particular automaker. Moment Energy has received investment from the Amazon Climate Pledge Fund, and Voyager Ventures, and has been awarded $20M+ from the DOE to assist in building the worlds first 2nd life giga factory.

name: Bramble Energy
one_liner: Bramble Energy is an innovator energy technology company that designs and manufactures high-performance, low-cost, and fuel cell stack.
description: Manufacturing operations platform within supply chain ecosystem - production, quality, or supplier management.
tags: Advanced Manufacturing / Cleantech / Consumer Electronics / Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Fuel Cells / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Hardware / Renewable Energy / Sustainability

name: ITM Power
one_liner: ITM Power designs and manufactures Hydrogen Energy Systems for Energy Storage and Clean Fuel production.
description:
ITM Power designs and manufactures Hydrogen Energy Systems for Energy Storage and Clean Fuel production based on the PEM Electrolyzer Technology.
ITM's principal motivation is to engineer zero-carbon hydrogen energy systems that provide energy security and independence from fossil fuels.
ITM's hydrogen systems are able to convert renewable energy to a clean fuel, that can be indefinitely stored for use in transport, residential and industrial applications. They have 4 products:
- HGas1SP
- HGas2SP
- HGas3SP
- HasXMW
These are  fully integrated turn-key system that are packaged in two separate ISO containers.
HGAS1SP is rated at ~0.65MW start of life (including all sub-systems) and is able to produce ~11kg of hydrogen per hour
HGAS2SP is rated at ~1.26MW start of life (including all sub-systems) and is able to produce ~22kg of hydrogen per hour.
HGas3SP is rated at ~2MW start of life (including all sub-systems) and is able to produce ~36kg of hydrogen per hour.
HgasXMW product is a large electrolyser system built based on modules of three ITM Power stacks:
- Each 3-stack module is built on a skid frame suitable to be housed indoors and is rated ~2MW capacity at beginning of life. 
- The number of 2MW modules in the system is scaled according to the hydrogen output or requirements.

name: gridX
one_liner: Leading platform provider building the infrastructure to connect and control distributed energy resources and enable the energy transition
description: gridX is a leading energy cloud scaleup founded by Andreas Booke and David Balensiefen with offices in Munich and Aachen. The company’s digital energy platform, XENON, provides scalable infrastructure to connect and control distributed energy resources. gridX uses edge and cloud services to develop digital solutions for smart charging and energy management in order to make renewable energy accessible to all, independent of manufacturers. These customizable solutions dynamically distribute available energy, intelligently optimize charging processes and ensure that peak loads are avoided.
tags: Analytics / Artificial Intelligence / AI / Big Data / Cloud / Cloud Computing / Cloud Security / Connected Home / Smart Home / Data Analytics / Digitalization / Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / EV Charging / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Hardware / Internet of Things / IoT / Platform / Predictive Analytics / Prescriptive Analytics / Pricing Analytics / Renewable Energy / Smart Buildings / Smart Home / Sustainability

name: Twaice
one_liner: Predictive analytics software for lithium-ion batteries using AI-powered digital twins
description:
Munich-based start-up TWAICE provides predictive analytics software that optimizes the development and operation of lithium-ion batteries. Our core technology is the digital twin - a software that combines deep battery knowledge and artificial intelligence to determine the condition and predict battery aging and performance. This makes complex battery systems more transparent, effective, and reliable. As the leading battery analytics software for players in the mobility and energy sectors, TWAICE is committed to increasing the lifetime, efficiency, and sustainability of the products that power the economy of tomorrow. We have proven the feasibility of our technology in numerous projects. Since June 2018, TWAICE has gained nine paying customers, ranging from automotive OEMs to power tool manufacturers and energy storage system (ESS) providers to insurance companies and testing institutions. TWAICE won the gold prize for the European Startup Prize for Mobility. 
The major strength of our team is the combination of academic excellence with business acumen. Our staff brings the perfect mix of expertise to the table: battery engineering, software development, data science, machine learning, and deep market understanding.
tags: Analytics / Artificial Intelligence / AI / Automotive / Connected Car / Data Analytics / Electrification / Predictive Analytics / Prescriptive Analytics

name: VoltStorage
one_liner: Manufactures eco-friendly Redox Flow battery systems for solar energy storage
description: VoltStorage develops and produces solar energy storage systems based on the eco-friendly Redox Flow technology to store surplus solar energy by day and use it during periods of low sunshine. With its sustainable energy storage solutions, VoltStorage follows the vision of making 100 percent renewable energy available around the clock – and thus making the world cleaner and fairer for future generations.
investors: EASME - EU Executive Agency for SMEs
tags: Battery / Battery Materials / Battery Membrane / Consumer Electronics / Distributed Energy / Electronics / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Hardware / Renewable Energy / Solid State Battery / Sustainability

name: Hoeller Electrolyzer
one_liner: Develops PEM electrolysis stacks for cost-efficient green hydrogen production
description: With its innovative PEM technology, HOELLER Electrolyzer GmbH is developing the future source of green hydrogen. The innovative PEM electrolysis stack Prometheus is the result of more than 25 years of expertise and pioneering work by engineer, managing director and head of development Stefan Höller. In these stacks, water is split up into hydrogen and oxygen by adding electricity. This makes them the core component of future electrolysis or power-to-x systems. With Prometheus, HOELLER is above all aiming to achieve convincing value for money, minimized hydrogen production costs, impressive maximum performance, outstanding service life and high hydrogen output pressure.

name: The WE LOVE ENERGY Label
one_liner: B2B2C platform providing a transparent renewable energy label that allows consumers to direct sustainable impact and utilities to offer differentiated green tariffs.
description: WE LOVE ENERGY offers a renewable energy label and B2B2C platform solution  that focuses on sustainability and consumer engagement. It is our mission to promote sustainability beyond the energy sector. We additionally finance sustainable projects that assist the UN´s Sustainable Development Goals (SDG´s). Our online platform allows consumers to direct the sustainable impact of their energy tariff by giving everyone an individual voice. This leads into a strong price-independent USP for utilities and a transparent value of renewable energy tariffs for consumers.
tags: B2B2C / Clean Label / Cleantech / Consumer Directed Benefits / Customer Engagement / Digitalization / Distributed Energy / Employee Engagement / Benefits / Platform / Renewable Energy / Social Impact / Sustainability

name: Phelas
one_liner: Develops containerized energy storage systems using air liquefaction for renewable energy operators.
description: Phelas was founded to make a very simple vision a reality: We want 100% renewable electricity to be an economic no-brainer everywhere on this planet. The Phelas team is developing Aurora, a standardized, mass-produced large-scale electrical storage system built on a new proprietary approach to air liquefaction with over 50% roundtrip efficiency. We use air and gravel as the main storage medium - both materials are universally available and, unlike lithium-ion, less hazardous and less resource-geographically dependent. The technology offers a decisive cost advantage for large amounts of energy, at the same time no cyclic degradation and excellent environmental compatibility. Since the storage system is based on already developed and reliable technology (compressors, cryotanks, turbine technology, etc.), the entire system can be operated extremely reliably and with low maintenance. The envisioned electricity storage system will consist of at least one power module with an electrical charge/discharge capacity of 1.5 or 1.2 MW, and at least one storage module with an electrical storage capacity of 2 MWh. Both modules will each be installed in 40-foot standard shipping containers to ensure high modularity and transportability.
tags: B2B / Energy Storage / Grid Storage / Energy Grid

name: ENT Biotech
one_liner: Digitized bioconversion: pharmaceutical ingredients from organic waste.
description: Agricultural robotics for farming automation, harvesting, or crop monitoring.
tags: Advanced Materials / Bio-Based Material / Bioplastics / Biotechnology / Pharmaceutical / Drugs / Pharmacy / Robotics

name: SOLAR MATERIALS GmbH
one_liner: Recovers silicon, silver, and glass from end-of-life solar panels using patented mechanical separation technology
description:
Solar (Photovoltaic, PV) energy is the backbone of Europe’s renewable energy supply. Recycling of end-of-life (EOL) solar modules is the missing link for a sustainable value chain. Our recycling process is the world’s first to economically recover all raw materials from end-of-life crystalline silicon solar modules. 

Through the energy-efficient recovery of all raw materials (including silicon, silver and high purity glass), we achieve a yield increase of over 250% compared to the current state of the art. The secondary raw materials we recover have an 80% lower carbon footprint than primary raw materials (based on material mix of solar modules). In our two-sided business model, we offer recycling as a service and sell the recovered raw materials to industry. While competitors have to take a recycling fee to be economical, we have the total recycling fee as a bargaining range to gain market share.
tags: Circular Economy / Cleantech / Recycling / Upcycling / Renewable Energy / Solar

name: suena GmbH
one_liner: AI-based operation and trading software for energy storage asset owners to maximize profits on short-term electricity markets.
description:
Short-term electricity storage is a decisive factor for the implementation of the energy transition. However, the possibility of temporally shifting electricity consumption and generation requires a new way of thinking when it comes to optimising energy flows.
Suena has developed an AI-based software platform for optimising the use and and trading optimisation software platform, which enables energy storage operators to adjust operators to maximise and stabilise their revenues in the short-term power and balancing markets. Maximise and stabilise their revenues.

After years of research in the field of renewable energy especially battery energy storage technology at the Technical University of Hamburg, the founder Dr. Lennard Wilkening, Tom Witter and Miguel Wesselmann put their research results into practice and developed the first cloud- and AI-based autopilot for energy storage systems. This not only increases efficiency but also cost-effectiveness with the goal of accelerating the energy transition.
tags: Artificial Intelligence / AI / Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Renewable Energy / Software / Software as a Service / SaaS / Sustainability

name: Etree mobility
one_liner: On-demand mobile charging service that brings quick-charge units to electric vehicles via app-based booking
description: The simple and impressive principle is this: simply have it charged. eTree electric vehicles are equipped with an innovative system of energy storage units and quick-charge technology. Using the app, costumers can request these vehicles at any time to charge their electric vehicle at their preferred time and place.
tags: Automotive / Connected Car / Renewable Energy / Smart Charging

name: HiLabs
one_liner: Develops ABBy, a rechargeable zinc-air battery for residential and industrial energy storage
description: The Haq Innovation LABoratorieS developed ABBy the rechargeable zinc-air battery. There is no directly comparable competitor product to this one. The lithium and cobalt-free HILABS ABBy battery with replaceable Electric Liquid and high TRL maturity is scalable. It is recyclable, inexpensive, safe and environmentally friendly. HILABS sees itself as a constant optimizer and developer of innovative solutions. ABBy is the flagship, which is perceived by experts as a potential game changer.

name: Syzygy Plasmonics
one_liner: Develops photoreactor technology that uses light-driven chemistry to produce zero-emission hydrogen and sustainable fuels without combustion.
description:
Syzygy is developing a next generation chemical reactor to produce low-cost hydrogen and other gases (e.g. methanol). Their reactor is powered by light instead of heat from burning fuel. It will operate at over 540°C lower temperature than conventional reactors, have reduced maintenance costs, be able to start or stop production near instantaneously, and have significantly reduced emissions.

The Syzygy photoreactor technology is the culmination of more than 30 years of plasmonics and nanotechnology research out of Rice University. Our combination of reactor design and photocatalytic nanoparticles enable light-driven chemical reactions at unprecedented efficiency. These disruptive innovations eliminate the need for combustion in the chemical reaction chain, enabling the production of zero-emission hydrogen, green ammonia, and other foundational chemicals. Our photoreactors are even capable of processing harmful carbon dioxide into high-value feedstocks like methanol, syngas, and formic acid.

name: ElectroActive Technologies
one_liner: Electro-Active Technologies is focused on transforming waste into renewable products.
description: Electro-Active Technologies is pushing a new paradigm for sustainable cities of the future where waste produced in cities, as well as renewable electricity, is used to generate zero-emission fuel for local use in the form of hydrogen. This can drastically reduce emissions from food waste in landfills as well as offsetting fossil fuel use through hydrogen use in fuel cells and other applications.
tags: Advanced Manufacturing / Biotechnology / Consumer Electronics / Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Hardware / Renewable Energy / Sustainability / Waste Reduction / Waste Management / Wearables

name: Sunfire
one_liner: Sunfire develops and manufactures systems for renewable industrial gas and fuel production.
description: Manufacturing robotics for factory automation, assembly, welding, or production operations.
tags: Advanced Manufacturing / Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Fuel Cells / Fuel Efficiency / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Industrial Robotics / Industry 4.0 / Industrial IoT / Renewable Energy / Sustainability

name: HiiROC
one_liner: HiiROC's modular, scalable technology generates zero-emission, low-cost hydrogen from natural gas, biomethane, flare gas or any other gaseous hydrocarbons.
description:
HiiROC has developed a process called Thermal Plasma Electrolysis, which uses electrical field in patented plasma torches to split apart hydrocarbons at high pressure, thus separating hydrogen and carbon in a continuous process.

HiiROC is uniquely able to utilize thermal-plasma technology through innovations in materials, geometry and engineering thereby operating at higher temperatures on a smaller scale and for longer. HiiROC has overcome critical issues associated with electrode erosion and carbon condensation and clogging.

Higher temperatures and operating at pressure also ensure full disassembly (rather than ionization) of the molecules and increases output efficiency. Until the HiiROC Solution, no-one else has successfully been able to resolve the critical issues of erosion and degradation of the electrode, (despite substantial scientific support) in the generation of hydrogen and carbon black from plasma. 
In the basic unit, 4x50kW plasma torches will be housed in a ~2m2/6m3 plant. Producing 432kg of Hydrogen at 50bar pressure per day.
tags: Air Quality / Carbon Capture / Distributed Energy / Fuel Cells / Mobility / Oil & Gas / Renewable Energy

name: Ekona Power
one_liner: Produces clean hydrogen and power from natural gas with carbon capture
description:
Existing approaches to producing hydrogen are either emissions-intensive, in the case of steam methane reforming, or expensive in the case of electrolysis with renewable energy.

Ekona is on a mission to solve this by creating a new solution to produce industrial scale hydrogen that is both low-cost and clean by converting natural gas into hydrogen, clean power, and pure CO2 that can be easily stored or used.
investors: British Columbia Innovative Clean Energy Fund
tags: Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Renewable Energy / Sustainability

name: C-Zero
one_liner: Decarbonizing Natural Gas.
description:
C-Zero is developing a process for removing the carbon found in natural gas, which will allow its customers to enjoy low-cost, on-demand energy without producing CO2.

The company’s proprietary process utilizes high temperature liquid catalyst systems to crack methane - the primary molecule in natural gas - into hydrogen and solid carbon. The solid carbon can be permanently sequestered, while the hydrogen can be sold as an industrial commodity or used to produce electricity and heat. When renewable natural gas is used, the company’s technology is carbon negative.

name: Mote
one_liner: Mote produces hydrogen by gasifying household trash.
description: Mote was founded with the goal of reducing greenhouse gas emissions in transportation. Today, Mote is building a core pillar of the clean energy future, producing negative-emission hydrogen fuel from waste.

name: Planetary Hydrogen
one_liner: Develops technology to produce hydrogen from ocean water while capturing atmospheric carbon dioxide
description:
Planetary Hydrogen’s patented system, dubbed SeaOH2, uses an electric current to split ocean water molecules into hydrogen and oxygen. 

Known as electrolysis, that process isn’t groundbreaking in itself. But by adding a mineral salt to the electrolysis cell, the company’s engineers have created an atmosphere-scrubbing compound called mineral hydroxide as a waste product. The hydroxide binds with carbon dioxide, effectively “capturing” the greenhouse gas while producing hydrogen as a potentially lucrative byproduct.

name: Monolith Materials
one_liner: Monolith is a next-generation, manufacturing company that utilizes clean, renewable electricity to transform natural gas into vital materials.
description: Manufacturing operations platform within supply chain ecosystem - production, quality, or supplier management.

name: Climeworks
one_liner: Capturing CO2 from the air.
description:
Climeworks is capturing CO2 from air with the world’s first commercial carbon removal technology. Climeworks' direct air capture plants removes CO2 from the atmosphere to supply to customers and to unlock a negative emissions future.

Climeworks built and operate the world’s first commercial direct air capture plant, removing 900 tonnes of CO2 from ambient air per year. Their plants capture atmospheric carbon with a filter, using mainly low-grade heat as an energy source. 

The pure CO2 gas is sold to customers in key markets, including: commercial agriculture, food and beverage industries, the energy sector and the automotive industry. Customers utilize this atmospheric CO2 in carbonated drinks or for producing carbon-neutral hydrocarbon fuels and materials. By using the captured CO2, customers can reduce their overall emissions as well as lowering their dependence on fossil energy. Their plants are modular, scalable and can be located independently of emission sources, allowing security of supply wherever there is atmospheric air.

Climeworks' plants can also be utilized for negative emissions. Compared to other carbon removal technologies, direct air capture does not depend on arable land, has a small physical footprint, and is fully scalable.
tags: Advanced Manufacturing / Artificial Intelligence / AI / Cleantech / Construction / Food Desert / Food Process / Smart Kitchen / Food Processing / Food Safety / Freshness / Functional Foods / Machine Learning / ML / Real Estate / Software / Software as a Service / SaaS / Sustainability

name: Versogen
one_liner: Producing green hydrogen at scale - reliably and affordably.
description:
The global energy transition to hydrogen is here, and Versogen (previously W7 Energy) is boldly leading the way.

Versogen's breakthrough electrolyzer technology uses water and renewable energy to produce green hydrogen at scale —reliably and affordably. Versogen's systems are built with patented anion exchange membranes (AEM) and earth-abundant materials. Their versatile hydrogen-generating solutions will substantially reduce industrial carbon emissions and lay the foundation for a sustainable energy future.

name: Antlia Energy
one_liner: A network for buying, selling, and trading green hydrogen.
description: Limiting climate change through deep decarbonization requires the rapid and cost-effective adoption of zero emissions fuels. Antlia Energy is the world’s first green hydrogen trading platform, connecting hydrogen consumers with green hydrogen producers. Green hydrogen market growth will rely on innovative deployment strategies. The Antlia platform creates a new class of fungible environmental assets through the decoupling of the “green” from green hydrogen. Antlia lowers the barrier for meeting net zero commitments by leveraging a system of certified, auditable, and tradable green hydrogen credits.

name: Lavo
one_liner: Australian company commercialising the world's first integrated hybrid hydrogen battery for residential and business energy storage.
description:
LAVO™ is a green energy technology and lifestyle company powered by Hydrogen. LAVO™ acts as a solar sponge, integrating with rooftop solar to capture and store renewable energy for use. Developed in partnership with UNSW and Design + Industry, the LAVO Hydrogen Energy Storage System (HESS) combines with rooftop solar to generate green hydrogen and store it safely in a patented hydride. The HESS is capable of storing and generating 40KWh of electricity.

LAVO Technology: 
- Creates Hydrogen from water
- Stores Hydrogen into LAVO™’s patented metal hydride
- Generates Electricity by converting hydrogen into power
- Delivers Power at a regulated voltage to your home
- Monitors & Controls performance via the LAVO™ app

name: CleanInnoGen Energy Solutions Ltd.
one_liner: CleanInnoGen decarbonizes heavy industry with on-site green hydrogen from waste heat.
description:
CleanInnoGen Energy Solutions Ltd. decarbonizes heavy industry with on-site production of green hydrogen and oxygen leveraging industrial waste heat. By commercializing a lab scale thermalchemical water splitting process, their disruptive solution only requires 15kWh/kg H2 which is up to 80% less compared to water electrolyzers. Green hydrogen and oxygen produced can be used as emission free fuel or feedstock for their customers which further lower the cost by minimizing transportation and storage cost. A wide range of heavy industry plants such as cement, steel and petrochemical will be ideal beachhead customers to take advantage of on-site low-cost green hydrogen and oxygen produced by their solution to decarbonize their processes.

Their solution will enable an important pathway to help the world achieve net-zero. In the future, it can go beyond waste heat and be powered by renewable heat sources such as solar heat or geothermal. 

A seed round is open now to support the execution and delivery of the first pilot with a multinational construction material company at its cement plant in Ontario, Canada to build the first-of-its-kind waste heat to hydrogen in the world.

name: Carbon 280
one_liner: Non-toxic hydrogen storage for long-duration renewable energy firming
description:
Carbon280 are commercializing the Hydrilyte Hydrogen Storage System. Unlike liquified hydrogen, compressed hydrogen, and ammonia, Hydrilyte is safe and non-toxic for people and the environment. 

Hydrilyte is is the most cost-competitive storage solution for renewable firming. On a renewable grid, powered by wind and/or solar PV, energy storage is required to “fill the gaps” when power from generation falls below the load. This is known as “renewable firming”. Where the gaps are in the order of seconds to hours, batteries and/or supercapacitors provide a cost-effective and technically efficient solution. However, batteries don’t have the energy density to economically meet longer term (> 4 hour) shortfalls. Fossil fuels are typically used to cover these longer periods that may extend to many days. Hydrogen is an alternative to fossil fuels that can help to fully decarbonize electricity power grids.

name: Wildfire Energy
one_liner: Converts residual waste into renewable energy, hydrogen and sustainable fuels using proprietary gasification technology
description: At Wildfire we produce renewable electricity, hydrogen and sustainable fuels from residual wastes destined for landfill such as domestic garbage and all types of biomass - generating carbon credits and having net negative emissions using our proprietary MIHG technology. MIHG (moving injection horizontal gasification) is a disruptive gasification approach that breaks down waste into constituent molecules of H2 and CO for re-assembly into sustainable fuels or use for energy. We have a working pilot plant, US and EU patents granted, earning $1MARR from blue-chip customers such as Naturgy, Calix, Hitachi & co-funding partners for our first commercial project. We have won many awards and completed many accelerators with investment from Katapult Climate fund and recently winning Petronas FutureTech3 in Asia.
tags: Biofuel / Cleantech / Renewable Energy / Sustainability / Waste Reduction / Waste Management

name: Vertus Energy
one_liner: Develops BRIO technology to convert waste into high-energy carbon-negative biofuel
description: Vertus Energy is accelerating the world’s transition to green energy. Its BRIO™ technology turbocharges the production of green fuel from waste and generates a more powerful biofuel, producing 60% more energy from the same amount of waste while also processing three times the amount of waste than any other system on the market.

name: Battolyser Systems
one_liner: Develops integrated battery-electrolyser systems for renewable energy storage and hydrogen production
description:
Imagine.. Always Clean Energy.

Battolyser Systems develops and manufactures the first integrated battery and electrolyser system a ‘Battolyser’ that can store electricity and produce hydrogen from renewable power to balance societal demand.
A Battolyser can store and supply electricity as a battery, and when it is fully charged, it automatically starts splitting water from the electrolyte into hydrogen and oxygen as an electrolyser. It can produce hydrogen when electricity prices are low and sell electricity when prices are high with an outstanding overall efficiency above 80%.
The technology is expected to be commercially available 2022 in The Netherlands followed large scale manufacturing capacity in 2023/24 onwards for global roll-out.
Battolyser B.V. is founded as a spin-off from Delft University of Technology in 2018, and is owned by its Founders and Koolen Industries and holds all related global patents and license rights.
tags: Battery / Battery Materials / Battery Membrane / Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Renewable Energy / Solid State Battery / Sustainability

name: Hystar
one_liner: Hystar specialises in ultra-efficient PEM electrolysers for the production of green hydrogen from water electrolysis.
description:
Hystar is an innovative, well financed, spin-off company  from SINTEF, one of Europe’s largest independent research  organizations. They are a small but diverse team of passionate people developing game-changing PEM electrolysers. They develop highly efficient PEM electrolysers, making the production of hydrogen from renewable electricity significantly more efficient. Green hydrogen is the key to making the energy transition happen. As a highly efficient energy carrier, green hydrogen has the potential to reduce greenhouse gas emission across many applications.

- Patented technology: Their systems utilize its patented PEM technology concepts, providing major steps towards making green hydrogen production more accessible for a multitude of applications.
-Modular and scalable: Their containerized systems are available in sizes from 1 to 5 MW. Through their modular and scalable design, they can provide plants up to 1 GW.
tags: Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage

name: Gravity Power
one_liner: A revolutionary grid-scale electricity storage system.
description: Gravity Power, LLC, a spin-off of LaunchPoint Technologies, Inc., is developing a revolutionary grid-scale electricity storage system. The company’s new Gravity Power Module(GPM) exploits the established principles of pumped storage hydropower, but extends the technology in a new direction: Down.

name: SPIN Storage Systems
one_liner: SPIN produces high efficiency flywheels that provide more economical and less environmentally harmful grid storage capacity than Li-ion or flow batteries.
description: SPIN is an energy storage company based in Silicon Valley. Their 30kWh/6kW carbon fiber flywheel has less than half the lifetime cost of Li-ion. It has no axle, eliminating balancing requirements, and only one moving part, the 400-pound rotor which spins 400x/second isolated in a vacuum. Zero maintenance, no toxic/strategic materials, non-flammable, and 100% recyclable. Units available early 2019.
tags: B2B / B2B2C / B2C / Cleantech / Enterprise / Hardware / Infrastructure / Large Enterprise / Smart Infrastructure

name: Gravitricity Limited
one_liner: Develops vertical-shaft gravity energy storage systems for grid-scale applications
description:
Grid-scale energy storage technology company based in Edinburgh. Founded by renewable energy veterans including inventor Peter Fraenkel. Partnered with global winch and EPC players to bring vertical-shaft energy storage to market.  
Sub-second response; zero degradation; cycle life >75,000: lowest LCOS on market for high-power applications.
tags: B2B / B2B2C / Cleantech / Hardware / Infrastructure / Smart Infrastructure

name: Natel Energy, Inc.
one_liner: Natel Energy is changing the way hydropower is built and developed to enable sustainable, climate resilient, low cost hydro and water AI.
description: Industrial IoT platform for supply chain visibility, monitoring, and connected operations.
tags: Advanced Manufacturing / Analytics / Artificial Intelligence / AI / B2B / B2B2C / Big Data / Cleantech / Data Analytics / Digitalization / Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Hardware / Industry 4.0 / Industrial IoT / Infrastructure / Predictive Analytics / Prescriptive Analytics / Pricing Analytics / Renewable Energy / Smart Infrastructure / Software as a Service / SaaS / Sustainability

name: Revterra
one_liner: Revterra is developing a highly efficient flywheel energy storage system for utility scale applications such as storage of wind and solar energy.
description: Revterra is developing a highly efficient energy storage system for long-duration, utility scale applications such as the storage of solar and wind energy. Current grid-scale energy storage technologies such as lithium-ion batteries make clean energy like solar and wind unclean due to their manufacturing methods and toxic byproducts. By introducing a highly efficient, low-cost alternative to lithium-ion batteries in industrial applications, Revterra will enable larger integration of renewables.  Their energy storage system is based on a high efficiency, passively stable magnetic bearing system which will enable their technology to reduce parasitic losses typically encountered in flywheel systems and to match the efficiency of lithium ion batteries while extending the lifespan by up to 10 times, thereby substantially reducing cost.
tags: Energy Storage / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Renewable Energy

name: H2GO Power
one_liner: Zero-emission solid-state hydrogen storage and hydrogen AI solutions.
description:
H2GO Power develops safe and low-cost hydrogen production and storage technology for zero-emissions, safe, and reliable power supply, with a range of commercial applications. 

H2GO Power's energy storage solution allows for clean, reliable and scalable energy storage for a wide range of commercial, industrial and residential applications. This includes applications where grid-based energy is either hard to achieve, unreliable, or uses considerable quantities of fossil fuel via conventional sources. Safe, solid-state proprietary technology eliminates the need for pressurizing hydrogen and unlocks significant commercial opportunities. With low pressure storage removing safety concerns of compressed hydrogen use, H2GO Power’s portable applications enable lightweight and clean energy storage. The is underpinned by a valuable IP portfolio with freedom to operate multiple applications the power and energy market.

H2GO's utility-scale storage units are the size of a standard shipping container and can be used in permanent or temporary cases worldwide. They are designed to take in renewable energy and store it as hydrogen for a long duration, with the unit able to release power on demand.
investors: Department for Energy Security & Net Zero
tags: Cleantech / Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Renewable Energy / Sustainability

name: Métalectrique
one_liner: Develops aluminium-air battery technology to power electric transport and remote locations with zero emissions.
description:
Métalectrique is a new way of powering transport and remote places. Our 21st century technology gives more mobility, costs less and is in harmony with the environment. We're working with other partners through our group companies to power transport and remote places with zero emissions. Aluminium-Air technology has long attracted attention due to its cheap, lightweight and high-energy nature. Aluminium is the most abundant metal on Earth and is easy to handle, store and is safe as a way of storing electrical energy. A Métalectrique power cell is actually quite simple. A piece of Aluminium is in contact with a special electrolyte. This electrolyte reacts with the metal on the negative end and on the positive end, air reacts with the electrolyte. When those two reactions are combined, aluminium is transformed into hydrated alumina and electrons are transferred from the second layer, through the conductor, ready to power a device. 

​
tags: Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Mobility / Renewable Energy

name: Celcibus
one_liner: Develops noble-metal-free catalysts and membrane electrode assemblies for fuel cells
description: Celcibus develops a patented catalyst to provide fuel cells with sustainable, high-performance, noble-metal-free power solutions. Its fuel cell solution for buildings simultaneously produces both heat and power. Celcibus’ products replace noble metals as the active sites in the fuel cell catalyst. This solves the problem of high costs and low control over mining conditions, as well as cumbersome chemicals recycling processes. Celcibus also develops a seven-layer membrane electrode assembly (MEA) that consists of a membrane coated with both cathode and anode catalysts and a gas distribution layer (GDL) on each side.

name: Ergosup
one_liner: Ergosup develops an innovative process for producing high pressure hydrogen on site for industrial applications, hydrogen service stations, as well as stationary massive energy storage solutions.
description:
ERGOSUP has developed an innovative process for the production of hydrogen under high pressure on site, allowing vehicles to be refueled with hydrogen fuel.

The ERGOSUP process offers the following decisive advantages, in terms of
- performance: the high hydrogen pressures required for filling vehicle tanks are supplied directly,
- safety: the production of oxygen is decoupled from that of hydrogen, and gas storage is minimized,
- savings: electricity consumption is targeted over optimal periods (excluding network peaks or availability of intermittent renewable electricity).

The target markets are the supply of decarbonated hydrogen for industrial applications, hydrogen service stations, as well as stationary massive energy storage solutions.

ERGOSUP, located in Drôme, is developing an innovative process for storing and converting energy. Already winner in January 2014 of the Seeding phase, she is also the winner of the second phase of the Concours Mondial de l'Innovation. The company is a member of the TENERRDIS.
tags: Biogas / Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Renewable Energy / Sustainability

name: Quidnet Energy
one_liner: Develops underground pumped hydro storage systems for grid-scale renewable energy
description:
Traditional pumped hydro storage is by far the largest and most cost-effective form of energy storage today. However, due to dependency on elevated terrain, pumped hydro storage could not be widely implemented, until Quidnet.

Quidnet Energy is a technology-driven energy company powering the carbon-free electric grid. The company is developing an alternative approach to energy storage by storing water to deliver energy. This new form of sub-surface pumped hydro storage enables large-scale deployment of renewable energy and allows for predictable, dispatchable delivery of power from intermittent renewable energy resources such as solar and wind. Quidnet’s energy system is widely deployable and integrates seamlessly with existing generating facilities.
investors: Jeremy and Hannelore Grantham Environmental Trust
tags: Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Renewable Energy / Sustainability

name: Renewell Energy
one_liner: They are repurposing oil and gas wells into the lowest cost, greenest, and most flexible energy storage in North America.
description: There are over 2 million inactive oil and gas wells in North America. Regulations vary, but in all cases owners are required to remediate them, a collective cost of over $280B. Renewell Energy is creating an energy storage device that can be used in place of remediation, allowing a huge impending expense to massively deploy gravity-based energy storage at a very low added cost. Renewell's solution will build the greenest, most flexible, and largest distributed energy storage network currently feasible. With a potential of over 40 GWhs at $62/kWh, Renewell can deliver the storage necessary for wind and solar to experience uninhibited market share growth.
tags: Distributed Energy / Energy Storage / Renewable Energy

name: ESS
one_liner: ESS is a leading provider of long-duration energy storage solutions ideally suited for C&I, utility, microgrid and off-grid applications.
description:
Since their founding in 2011, ESS has been focused on laying the groundwork for a future powered by clean and reliable energy.

With a team that boasts decades of experience in distributed power generation and energy storage technologies, ESS has developed an extremely cost-effective energy management system that combines a safe, abundant and non-toxic iron electrolyte with their patented flow cell design. This combination of high performance with low cost means that ESS’s technology is ideally suited for applications that range in size from retail energy management to utility-scale renewables integration.
investors: Export-Import Bank of the United States
tags: Consumer Electronics / Distributed Energy / Electronics / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Hardware / Renewable Energy / Sustainability

name: Energy Vault
one_liner: Energy Vault offers a utility-scale, gravity-based storage solution for renewable energy.
description:
Energy Vault offers an energy storage technology utilizing fundamental principles of science to deliver a storage solution that's as compassionate to the planet as it is efficient to build and operate. The Energy Vault solution utilizes the same fundamentals of physics and kinetic energy as pumped hydro but replaces the water with custom made cylindrical blocks utilizing an extremely innovative use of low-cost materials.

The system is modular and can be designed with a capacity of between 10 and 35MWh and a power output of up to 5MW. The system has been designed for a 30+ year life with no degradation in storage capacity.
tags: Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Renewable Energy / Sustainability

name: Ashlawn Energy
one_liner: Manufactures VanCharg™ energy storage systems for grid, commercial, and residential applications
description:
Ashlawn Energy has produced VanCharg. VanCharg is an electrical storage system (battery) that stores enough electricity to provide back-up power to hundreds of homes and support the transmission and distribution of electricity. VanCharg reduces electricity costs and cuts carbon emissions by allowing customers to 'beat the peak' and store less expensive and less polluting power produced at night for use during peak hours.

VanCharg safely holds megawatt hours of electricity for milliseconds to months and has been called the 'missing link' required to safely connect massive solar and wind farms to the electrical grid.
tags: Energy Storage / Grid Storage / Energy Grid

name: H2Pro
one_liner: H2Pro develops and manufactures highly efficient hydrogen generators using novel electrochemical technology.
description:
H2Pro is a developing an innovative green hydrogen production technology based on a disruptive process called E-TAC (Electrochemical — Thermally-Activated Chemical) water splitting. Like electrolysis, E-TAC uses electricity to split water – however it decouples hydrogen and oxygen production, achieving unprecedented efficiency (95%). As oxygen and hydrogen are not generated at the same time, no membrane is required, making the process inherently safer, and simplifying construction and scale-up. Moreover, hydrogen can be produced at high pressure.

H2Pro will release its first product, with a 500 kg/day capacity for hydrogen refueling stations, in 2023. Larger, multi-ton systems expected soon after, intended for industrial and energy applications (ammonia, steel, energy storage).
tags: Climate Change / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Storage

name: EH Group Engineering
one_liner: EH Group Engineering is focused on the development and production of its innovative fuel cell technology
description: Manufacturing operations platform within supply chain ecosystem - production, quality, or supplier management.

description: Construction robotics for building automation, site monitoring, or heavy equipment automation.

name: Coval Energy
one_liner: Develops modular reactors for electro-catalytic CO2 reduction to produce renewable chemicals and bio-fuel feedstocks
description: Coval Energy developed a high-pressure modular reactor which enables efficient electro-catalytic reduction of CO2 to produce renewable chemicals, including potassium formate and formic acid. Potassium formate can in turn be used as a feedstock for bio-based processes to create lipids and fatty acid chains for fuels, and proteins for aquaculture.
tags: Bio-Based Material / Carbon Capture / Chemicals / Material Processing

name: Duralite Power
one_liner: Develops portable hydrogen fuel cell power systems for long-endurance drones and industrial applications
description: There is an ever-growing demand for long endurance and clean energy in today's society. We aim to address this demand by utilising fuel cell and hydrogen technology. DuralitePower develops portable Fuel Cell Stacks in the range of 200W to 1kW for a range of applications. Alongside, DuralitePower also develops Drone Power Systems, specializing in long-endurance, fuel cell based power systems for fixed-wing and multi-rotor drones. Finally, Duralite Power also provides customised solutions tailored to the needs of their customer.

name: HeatVentors
one_liner: Builds phase-change material thermal energy storage units for district heating, cooling, and data centers
description:
HeatVentors developed HeatTank, a phase-change-material (PCM) based thermal energy storage unit to increase the efficiency of district heating and cooling, as well as the thermal management of data centres.

For its district heating and cooling application, HeatVentor’s solution effectively replaces water storage, which reduces the system’s size by 60% and brings energy efficiency gains between 20-40%. HeatVentor’s USP is therefore an enhanced peak load management, space optimization, and security of thermal energy supply.
tags: Advanced Materials / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Grid Storage / Energy Grid

name: Terrapin Geothermics
one_liner: Global Waste Heat to Power Project Developers
description:
Terrapin Geothermics Inc. (Terrapin) is a designer and developer of industrial heat recovery projects. As a turn-key solutions provider, Terrapin helps large industrial emitters advance their net-zero commitments by generating emission-free, baseload power from wasted heat off their operating assets. 

Unlike the traditional project developer, Terrapin will self-finance and build turnkey clean energy projects at zero cost to the client where we will pay them for access to turn their waste heat into value. Terrapin can provide the generated carbon offset credits as a royalty payment for access to their waste heat which offers rare opportunity to receive emission reductions for $0 per tCO2 reduced or clients can buy our emission-free power behind the fence at a reduced $/kWh rate.

Terrapin's team is comprised of energy and utilities experts who specialize in developing the operational, commercial, technical, and regulatory aspects of heat recovery projects. Ultimately, Terrapin's projects represent a negative-cost, emissions reduction opportunity for industrial facility operators.
tags: Distributed Energy / Electrification / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Renewable Energy / Sustainability

name: Climeon
one_liner: Converts low-temperature waste heat into clean electricity
description:
Using a modified version of the Organic Rankine Cycle (ORC), HeatPower exploits the temperature difference between a hot and cold source and uses an environmentally friendly working media to generate clean energy.

Working independently of weather conditions, HeatPower delivers baseline, renewable power and safeguards your access to sustainable energy.

As a modular and scalable solution, HeatPower systems can be customized depending on the amount of thermal energy available and enhanced with additional upgrades to increase power production and optimize outputs.

name: AEInnova
one_liner: AEInnova repurposes industrial waste heat creating negative carbon energy to power. Their product is a battery-less IoT devices that are free of maintenance.
description: Industrial IoT and connected manufacturing systems for smart factory operations.
investors: EASME - EU Executive Agency for SMEs
tags: Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Industry 4.0 / Industrial IoT / Internet of Things / IoT

name: EthosGen
one_liner: EthosGen develops eco-friendly, renewable energy systems by converting waste heat into renewable energy or by developing solar projects.
description: EthosGen LLC, a Wilkes-Barre, PA-based developer of an on-site alternative energy waste-heat-to-electrical-power generator. EthosGen’s Heat Absorption technology and or Biosolids to Biochar technologies were designed to give manufacturers power over their wasted heat. Often exhausted into the atmosphere, EthosGen has developed technology to make use of this precious resource so that manufacturing companies can save money while becoming more sustainable.
tags: Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Renewable Energy / Software / Software as a Service / SaaS / Solar / Sustainability

name: Poligy
one_liner: Poligy develops solutions that convert waste heat into electricity.
description:
Poligy offers solutions for low temperature waste heat. Poligy develops heat engines that are powered by their patented material - the Bipolymer. This innovative material is the principle that allows them to effectively convert heat into electricity at a fraction of the current market price. 

Based in Dusseldorf, Poligy's solutions can increase the energy efficiency of all energy intensive processes, e.g. energy plants, chemical manufacturing, steel and cement production, etc. 

To achieve Poligy's goal of building a sustainable future, they are also currently working on a passive data cooling solution that would reduce up to 50% of a data center's operational costs and replacing the need for energy intensive cooling systems.

name: SoundEnergy
one_liner: Develops thermoacoustic cooling systems that convert waste heat or solar energy into cooling without refrigerants, compressors, or electricity
description: SoundEnergy has a disruptive cooling technology that allows the efficient conversion of heat into cooling without the use of harmful chemicals or refrigerants, a compressor, or moving parts. This means they can recover otherwise wasted energy to displace electricity used in cooling or use solar heat to do the same in extreme conditions. This translates into massive reductions in environmental impact and CO2 emissions as well as energy costs and maintenance costs associated with electric chillers.
investors: EASME - EU Executive Agency for SMEs
tags: Building Maintenance / Cleantech / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization

name: Viking Cold Solutions
one_liner: Viking Cold Solutions provides energy management systems and thermal energy storage solutions to help companies achieve energy efficiency.
description:
Cold storage and frozen food facilities are highly energy intensive and exposed to volatile electricity pricing, demand charges, and grid instability. Managing peak load while preserving strict temperature requirements is critical to protecting food quality and controlling operating costs.

Viking Cold Solutions is a thermal energy management company that enables cold storage operators to store energy as cold and use it when power is most economical. Its systems are deployed directly inside frozen food production, storage, and distribution facilities to improve flexibility, reduce costs, and maintain temperature stability.

The company uses long duration thermal energy storage systems based on environmentally friendly phase change materials combined with cloud based intelligent controls. These systems have no mechanical components and leverage existing refrigeration equipment to shift load, manage demand, and integrate solar energy by storing excess generation as cold.

Commercially, Viking Cold delivers a levelized cost of energy below two cents per kilowatt hour, materially lowering energy expenses for cold chain operators. Strategically, the solution addresses grid constraints and renewable intermittency while improving sustainability without disrupting core facility operations.
tags: Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Renewable Energy / Sustainability

name: Water Horizon
one_liner: Water Horizon develops a mobile thermal battery to recover waste heat from industrial activities and distribute it at another time and in another place to a consumer as renewable heat or cold.
description:
Water Horizon is a French start-up created in 2017 by Jean-Emmanuel Faure.
The company's goal is to develop an innovative renewable energy solution for industrial heating and cooling applications. The technology is a mobile thermal battery. It recovers waste heat from industrial activities. This heat is currently unused and lost.

Once absorbed, the heat is stored without loss, allowing it to be transported and distributed at another time.

This decoupling, both spatial and temporal, offers a real opportunity to reuse lost heat that is far from consumption areas or that cannot follow variations in demand.

Therefore, WH makes renewable energy profitable for companies and enable them to reduce GHG, promotes positive energy for the territory, and on a larger scale WH contributes to achieve national environmental objectives.

Water Horizon has developed the first mobile thermal battery capable of supplying renewable energy in a reversible form: hot or cold.
tags: Chemicals / Circular Economy / Clean Label / Cleantech / Climate Change / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / New Products / Renewable Energy / Sustainability / Waste Reduction / Waste Management

name: Kanin Energy
one_liner: Kanin Energy design an efficiency technology to help heavy industries to monetize their waste heat and decarbonize their operations. Converting CO2 to energy.
description:
Kaning energy is a development company that focuses on transforming industrial waste heat into emission-free power with no capital needed from host facilities.

Kanin brings together technology, engineering, construction and third-party capital to turn wasted energy into revenue for our industrial partners. We build independent power plants that allow our partners to reduce carbon emissions with no capital expenditures. Industrial partners can monetize their waste heat and not worry about plant construction, financing or operations.
tags: Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Industry 4.0 / Industrial IoT / Renewable Energy / Sustainability

name: i-TES Thermal Energy Storage
one_liner: Develops bio-based phase change material (PCM) thermal batteries for efficient heat and cold storage
description:
i-TES is a startup established in December 2016 by three partners, two operating in the company and one external.

The company structure has grown until today to have increased the company structure first with the entry of Alens (Company specialized in energy consulting) and then with the multi-utility Iren. Like the partners, the team has also increased and four junior resources have been gradually added. 

The core business of i-TES is the study, design and marketing of innovative thermal storage batteries because they are based on phase change materials, better known as PCM. Their new way of managing thermal energy (heat), makes these materials perfect substitutes for the water contained in traditional thermal storage tanks. Thermal batteries are ideal when there is a decoupling between thermal energy production and use, and are therefore widely used in combination with heat pumps, solar thermal, district heating and can also be used to recover waste heat in production processes.
tags: Advanced Materials / Battery / Battery Materials / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Waste Reduction / Waste Management

name: HyperBorean
one_liner: Converting waste heat to free cooling.
description: Manufacturing operations platform within supply chain ecosystem - production, quality, or supplier management.
tags: Advanced Manufacturing / Automotive / Connected Car / Cleantech / Consumer Electronics / Electronics / Hardware / Sustainability / Transportation

name: Phase Change Solutions
one_liner: Phase Change Solutions is a global leader in temperature control and energy-efficient solutions, using phase change materials.
description:
Phase Change Solutions (PCS) is a global leader in the development and supply of phase change materials (“PCMs”). The company’s proprietary family of PCMs, collectively referred to as BioPCM®, has been certified with 100% bio-based content and listed among the USDA Biopreferred products. BioPCM® can be designed to store and release thermal energy at temperatures ranging from -75°C to 175°C. This tuning capability enables customers worldwide to use BioPCM® to reach their sustainable development goals (SDGs). 

Since its founding in 2011, PCS has helped maintain optimum temperatures and save energy in a range of industries: transportation and storage of food, pharmaceuticals, and perishables; buildings and structures; and telecom and data centers.

The company continues to be recognized globally for its scientific achievements, innovative products, and approach to sustainability. PCS was most recently selected as one of nine companies in the inaugural cohort of The Clean Fight New York.
tags: Advanced Manufacturing / Advanced Materials / Architecture / Chemicals / Cleantech / Delivery / Last Mile Logistics / Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Renewable Energy / Smart Buildings / Smart Home / Sustainability

name: Rondo Energy
one_liner: Develops thermal batteries that store renewable electricity as high-temperature heat for industrial processes.
description:
The industrial sector, which includes everything from cement and glass manufacturing to food and beverage production to the energy industry itself, needs continuous, high-temperature and cheap heat. Today, only fossil fuels can meet these needs, and indeed industrial energy accounts for more than 1/3 of global CO2.

Rondo replaces fossil fuels with clean electricity and thermal energy storage. Rondo’s patent-pending technology rapidly converts clean electricity to heat, and delivers clean industrial heat at lower cost than fossil fuels. This means that Rondo units can directly replace fossil fuel-fired equipment and deliver heat in various forms to meet a wide range of industrial needs 24/7 - including those of power generation, cement, and metals - with very little energy loss.

The Rondo Heat Battery charges intermittently from wind, solar, or the grid. Electricity uniformly heats bricks made from safe, widely available materials. Stored heat is delivered on demand at temperatures up to 1200 celsius.

name: Altergo
one_liner: AI-powered battery digital twin platform for optimizing performance and reducing costs in electric vehicles and energy storage systems
description:
Altergo is a battery science platform that combines digital twins and AI to elevate battery performance, reduce costs and deliver exceptional customer experience. 

The platform allow users to implement recommendations and integrate insights into mission critical applications, perform predictions and simulations based on proprietary cutting-edge AI/ML and semi empirical models. Thanks to the architecture of the platform group of battery digital twins can be created to support deployment scenarios and maintenance strategies. Digital twins that are related through complex hierarchical relationships in physical systems can be created through the platform. The architecture of the platform provides data ingestion capabilities (e.g. data ingestion APIs). 

The platform capabilities include: 

- Battery digital twin creation
- Warranty and performance management
- Reporting and visualisation
- SDK & API integration
- State of Health and State of Function estimation
- Function manager

name: Relectrify
one_liner: Relectrify supplies advanced control technology, which increases the lifetime and decreases the cost of battery storage systems, both for second-life and new batteries.
description: Relectrify is a developer and supplier of advanced battery management and inverter solutions. Their cell-level, distributed technology demonstrably increases battery life and reduces cost in energy storage solutions, including for households, industry and the power grid. The company was spun out of battery research at the University of Melbourne, Australia in 2015. Supported by leading energy investors, Relectrify’s technology has attracted interest by major energy storage manufacturers and installers globally, including American Electric Power, Nissan US, VW Group Germany, Vector New Zealand and 4R Energy Japan. Relectrify is actively engaging with strategic collaborators to bring to market more affordable and sustainable storage products.

name: Hydrogen In Motion
one_liner: Develops proprietary nano-materials for safe, portable hydrogen storage tanks that operate at ambient conditions and low pressures
description:
A Canadian R&D company that develops new and novel methods of storing hydrogen gas. Their first product, the H2M Genesis tank will be based on a proprietary nano-material that adsorbs and releases hydrogen at ambient conditions and at pressures under 50 bar. The H2M Genesis is the first of a series of hydrogen storage materials under development by H2M. Each iteration will target significant ground-breaking improvements in terms of volume, weight and performance.

H2M technology removes the need for energy-intensive equipment, high pressure, and high temperature while improving safety and capacity at a lower cost. H2M tanks can be used in three major ways: transport and distribute hydrogen; improve hydrogen fuel cell refuelling; and store energy generated from other sources of energy (ex: solar, wind).

name: Compact Carbon Capture
one_liner: Compact CO2 capture technology reducing equipment size by 75% for industrial applications
description:
3C is a groundbreaking new technology for CO2 capture. The technology allows for capturing CO2 even where a limited amount of space is available.

We are reducing the size of the equipment by 75%, without loosing efficiency or increasing pressuredrop! The reduction in size will of course also significantly reduce the CAPEX for CO2 capture.

name: Cambridge Carbon Capture
one_liner: Develops carbon capture technology that mineralizes industrial CO2 emissions into permanent rock form while generating valuable by-products
description:
Cambridge Carbon Capture Ltd is developing a technology solution to Industrial CO2 Capture & Storage/Utilisation, based upon IP-protected advanced CO2 mineralisation technology. CCC’s technology has the potential to strip CO2 from industrial flue gases for less than the current carbon price, with the potential to offset this cost or even make a profit from sale of semi-precious metals and other mineral by- products from the process.

Their patented CO2LOC technology offers a solution to climate change by enabling industry to profitably sequester CO2 through a two-stage mineralization process. The mineralization process permanently locks the sequestered CO2 in rock form and due to its flexibility, can be utilized across a range of industries. CO2LOC is unique in that it approaches the planet’s dependency on fossil fuels by introducing a profit motive to industry to invest in emission-reduction. Their advanced mineralisation processes combine low cost inputs to de-carbonise the emissions, whilst simultaneously producing valuable by-products.
