name: Ziptrax Cleantech
one_liner: Develops AI-powered tools to segregate usable Li-ion cells and extracts valuable metals including lithium, cobalt, nickel, and manganese from spent batteries using hydrometallurgical processes.
description:
Ziptrax CleanTech develops Smart City Solutions by utilizing Closed Loop recycling for putting the Li-ion Batteries from Energy Storage Systems into 2nd Use or New Cell Manufacturing. We help the conservation and re-use of metals such as Lithium, Cobalt, Nickel and Manganese from the Spent Li-ion batteries for powering future clean technologies like EV Storages, Renewable Energy Grids and Consumer electronics. Closed Loop recycling eliminates the Li-ion batteries from waste dumps and landfills and removes the environmental impacts from the Li-ion batteries.

We are currently raising our Series A round of 1.5M.
tags: Advanced Materials / Artificial Intelligence / AI / Asset Tracking / B2B / Battery / Battery Materials / Circular Economy / Cleantech / Climate Change / Data Analytics / Digital Twin / EV / Electronic Vehicles / Intellectual Property / IP / Predictive Maintenance / Recycling / Upcycling / Sustainability / Waste Reduction / Waste Management

name: Dimensional Energy
one_liner: Fuel for the future made from carbon dioxide with sunlight.
description:
Dimensional Energy is a chemistry company pioneering artificial photosynthesis to produce environmentally responsible polymers and chemical intermediaries. They're s making solar fuels viable through efficient utilization and even distribution of sunlight in scalable reactor forms with novel, functionalized catalysts.

In 2021, they've been scaling their work from XPRIZE 10x in Tucson, Arizona and adding all the necessary processes to produce 20 gallons of ASTM certified Jet A fuel per day. With a  projected conversion rate of 75%, dropping the price per gallon of fuel to $27 per gallon. Their next installation in 2023 will include a six megawatt heliostat field and produce 1000 gallons of fuel per day at a price of $2.70 per gallon. This fuel will be sold to an airline that will be announced shortly.

Their key ingredient is sunlight. They utilize its energy for process heat and electricity. Next they add carbon dioxide, which can be accepted from any source—air, factories or pipelines. The only other input is water, from which we make hydrogen via electrolysis. Inside the reactor, light- and heat-activated catalysts initiate the conversion of CO2 and H2 into an energy-dense synthesis gas. This “syngas” forms the basis of many of the world’s most-used products.

name: Natron Energy
one_liner: Develops high-power, long-cycle-life sodium-ion batteries using Prussian blue chemistry for industrial energy storage and EV fast charging applications.
description:
Natron Energy is an energy storage company that develops a high power, long cycle life, and low-cost battery technology. Natron’s battery products are based on a unique Prussian blue chemistry and can integrate into a wide variety of energy storage applications ranging from critical backup power systems to EV fast charging and behind-the-meter applications.

Natron’s patented Prussian blue electrodes store and transfer sodium-ions faster, more often, and with lower internal resistance than any other commercial battery. With zero strain during charge / discharge, 10x faster cycling, and an over 50,000 cycle-life Natron’s sodium-ion batteries represent the future of industrial mobility. Sodium-ion batteries take advantage of standard lithium-ion pouch cell production lines while benefiting from a more sustainable chemistry. Natron’s cells do not rely on rare earth materials like cobalt nor do they have a long, questionable supply chain like lithium. Made from commodity materials including aluminum, iron, manganese, and sodium-ions (~68% of the earth’s surface is covered with salt water) Natron’s cells, modules, and battery represent a responsible alternative to lead and lithium batteries.
tags: Battery / Battery Materials / Battery Membrane / Cleantech / Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Renewable Energy / Solid State Battery / Sustainability

name: Standard Energy
one_liner: Develops vanadium ion batteries for grid-scale energy storage systems
description:
Standard Energy is a South Korea-based tech startup and developer of vanadium ion battery that is on the verge of revolutionizing the current lithium ion-based energy storage system.

Their battery is specially designed for ESS and the solution includes Energy Management System (EMS), Battery Management System (BMS), and Power Conditioning System (PCS). They have Universal Battery for all Ranges of ESS - Full-range scalability of ESSs from the mAh-class to the massive MWh-class and Seamless compatibility with commercial power systems.

one_liner: With the ability to capture CO2 directly from industrial sources at less than half the capital cost of existing solutions, Svante makes commercial-scale carbon capture reality, and positions global industries to play offense in the fight against climate change.
description: Technology company classified under robotics / Industrial Automation taxonomy.
tags: Carbon Capture / Cleantech / Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Industrial Robotics / Industry 4.0 / Industrial IoT / Renewable Energy / Sustainability

name: SunGreenH2
one_liner: Revolutionizing green hydrogen generation from electrolysis with the world’s highest performance advanced materials.
description:
SunGreenH2 is transforming green hydrogen production by building the world's highest performance electrolyzers using their platform technology to incorporate proprietary advanced nanostructured materials into electrolyzer components. Electrolyzers made with their materials increase production 2x and decrease energy consumption 10% using 30x lower amount of expensive platinum group metals.‍ 

Their novel, ground-breaking technology is a result of over 10 years of cutting-edge research and innovation in electrochemistry and nanotechnology for renewable energy generation. They have a vision for decarbonising the planet with zero carbon, low cost, renewable H2. 

They use only sustainable, earth abundant materials and their fabrication method is compatible with existing industrial processes allowing the technology to scale from KW-MW-GW scale systems. They have successfully demonstrated a working prototype of their electrolysis cell utilizing their proprietary components. Their aim is to seek strategic partners to commercialize their electrolyzer components and mass-deploy them across all types of electrolyzers. 

In future their platform technology can be used to make other green transition materials for fuel cells, batteries, hydrogen compression, storage and solar-hydrogen panels.

name: McPhy
one_liner: Provides turnkey hydrogen production, storage and distribution solutions for energy, mobility and industrial applications.
description:
As a leading supplier of hydrogen production, storage and distribution equipment, McPhy Energy contributes to the deployment of clean hydrogen throughout the world. McPhy Energy provides turnkey solutions to its clients, tailored to their applications: Hydrogen energy (renewable energy surplus storage and valorization) Mobility (car refueling) Industrial hydrogen (raw material for industrial sites of all sizes and all sectors)

In the framework of the energy revolution, and as a leading supplier of hydrogen production and distribution equipment, McPhy  contributes to the deployment of clean hydrogen throughout the world.

name: SOLETAIR POWER
one_liner: Develops direct air capture systems that remove CO2 from indoor air to improve cognitive function and from outdoor air for industrial use.
description:
Finnish company Soletair Power is in the business of direct air capture (DAC), sucking CO₂ from ventilators inside buildings and capturing it to improve air quality. Rather than just reducing carbon emissions, its sell is that by capturing CO₂ indoors, DAC can improve employees’ cognitive function per 400 parts per million (ppm) CO₂ removed by 20%, according to a study by Harvard. 

For industrial emitters and for those needing CO2 in the purest form as a product, their system captures CO2 outdoors as well. CO2 is decreased from the atmosphere and this has a direct positive impact on stopping climate change.

The startup sold its first commercial unit in February 2020 — although it won’t disclose who its customers are. However, it is backed by 187-year-old ship engine and power plant maker Wartsila, which raised its $600k seed funding round in 2019.

name: ZEG Power
one_liner: Creating carbon zero hydrogen through carbon sequestration.
description:
ZEG delivers solutions for clean hydrogen production from gas, through its novel ZEG ICC™ Technology with integrated carbon capture. Combined with turbine or fuel-cell, ZEG can deliver solutions for clean power production. ZEG is currently in the process of building the first commercial ZEG hydrogen production plant in Norway. This could mean the beginning of the end for Norway as large-scale exporter of climate emissions, at the same time as we preserve and ensure industrial job creation in Norway.

ZEG Clean Hydrogen Solutions enables clean hydrogen from natural gas due to the innovative ZEG ICC™ Technology with integrated CO₂ capture. This solution is within the 'blue hydrogen' category. ZEG's aim is to provide an improved solution (a 'better box') compared with other blue hydrogen solutions, due increased CO2 capture rate, increased overall efficiency, non-toxic carbon capture sorbent, and a reduction in plant footprint.

In addition to natural gas and other hydrocarbon gases, ZEG plants can be based on locally available, biomass resources (gasified biomass, or biogas). ZEG Climate+ Solution™ is based on biogas, enabling production of hydrogen and/or electric power with negative emissions. Negative emissions mean that an activity is a net remover of atmospheric CO₂.

name: Heirloom
one_liner: Develops cost-effective direct air capture technology using carbon mineralization to permanently remove CO2 from the atmosphere
description:
Naturally occurring minerals - alongside forests, soils, and the ocean - are one of our planet's most vital carbon sinks. Over geological timescales, carbon dioxide in the air and water chemically bind to these minerals and permanently turn to stone.

Heirloom's technology enhances this natural process, called carbon mineralization, to help minerals absorb CO2 from the ambient air in days, rather than years. By combining the best of engineering and nature, they can offer the most cost-effective and scalable Direct Air Capture solution in the world.

name: Power to Hydrogen
one_liner: Power to Hydrogen is bringing down the cost of clean hydrogen by designing AEM electrolyzers that reduce precious metal needs by 90%, integrate behind the meter with intermittent renewables, and produce the hydrogen at 250 bar to reduce transportation costs.
description:
Power to Hydrogen designs and manufactures advanced AEM electrolyzer technology that enables cost-effective, renewable hydrogen production. The company focuses on eliminating the core technical and economic barriers that limit clean hydrogen adoption, providing a solution that aligns directly with industrial decarbonization, synthetic fuels, and long-duration storage needs. Their mission is to accelerate the transition to a clean energy economy by making green hydrogen more affordable, scalable, and compatible with real-world renewable generation.

Their AEM electrolyzer platform addresses three major industry challenges. First, it operates reliably with intermittent renewable power, which allows utilities and developers to pair hydrogen production directly with solar and wind assets. Second, it replaces expensive and supply-constrained materials like platinum, iridium, and titanium with nickel and stainless steel, dramatically reducing CAPEX and supply chain risk. Third, the system produces hydrogen at 250 bar, the pressure required for transportation, liquefaction, or ammonia production, eliminating the need for external compression and reducing both energy use and cost.

Backed by industry leaders such as Shell, NASA, and ARPA-E, the company has built strong technical and commercial validation. Power to Hydrogen has deployed revenue-generating pilots with major global utilities, including Enel Green Power, American Electric Power, EDP, E.ON, and ESB. These early deployments demonstrate system durability, operational efficiency, and integration with real-world industrial and renewable settings.

The technology is now scaling toward its first industrial-scale demonstration at a major European port. With its efficient, durable, high-pressure AEM design, Power to Hydrogen offers a practical pathway for industrial clients, hydrogen hubs, and energy developers seeking lower-cost hydrogen that can be produced where and when it is needed.
tags: Advanced Materials / Chemicals / Cleantech / Distributed Energy / Energy Storage / Fuel Cells / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Oil & Gas / Sustainability

name: Allegro Energy
one_liner: Develops redox flow battery systems for long-duration energy storage using patented microemulsion electrolyte technology.
description:
Allegro Energy is an advanced energy storage company pioneering next-generation solutions for long-duration energy storage (LDES) across industrial and grid-scale applications. As renewable energy penetration increases, the need for reliable, scalable storage becomes critical to maintaining grid stability and enabling continuous clean power. Allegro addresses this challenge with a Redox Flow Battery Energy Storage System (RFBESS) designed to deliver safe, cost-effective, and durable energy storage at scale.

At the core of Allegro’s platform is its patented microemulsion electrolyte technology, which leverages abundant, widely available materials to achieve higher performance than traditional single-solvent redox flow batteries. This approach improves energy density, efficiency, and operational stability while avoiding the supply chain constraints and safety risks associated with lithium-based systems. Allegro’s batteries are non-flammable, non-toxic, and aligned with corporate sustainability and regulatory requirements.

Allegro’s RFBESS is purpose-built for long-duration use cases, enabling energy storage over extended time horizons that support grid balancing, renewable integration, and industrial energy management. The system’s modular design allows for flexible deployment across a wide range of scales, from localized industrial facilities to utility-scale installations, while maintaining consistent performance and reliability.

With global demand for long-duration energy storage projected to exceed 140 TWh by 2040, Allegro is positioned to scale rapidly alongside the energy transition. The company’s technology is engineered for large-scale manufacturing, enabling exponential production growth as adoption accelerates. By combining technical innovation with manufacturing scalability, Allegro Energy is setting a new standard for safe, sustainable, and economically viable long-duration energy storage.
investors: Origin Energy; The Grantham Foundation; The Melt; ...
tags: Battery / Battery Materials / Cleantech / Energy Storage / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Sustainability

name: Electric Hydrogen
one_liner: Builds electrolyzers to produce green hydrogen for industrial decarbonization
description:
Founded by experienced renewable energy technologists and backed by committed mission-driven investors, Electric Hydrogen is building solutions to help cost-effectively decarbonize the massive industrial sectors that we all rely on. 

Electric Hydrogen is building the world’s most efficient and low cost electrolyzers to produce green hydrogen from water and renewable energy at truly global scale. By completely re-imagining the design and manufacture of hydrogen electrolysis systems matched to renewable power sources, Electric Hydrogen's mission is to generate truly green hydrogen for a post-carbon world.

name: H2E Power Systems
one_liner: Develops fuel cell systems that convert hydrocarbon fuels into clean electricity for stationary power applications
description:
H2E Power Systems is a cleantech company that develops technology solutions that help achieve energy independence and energy security. The h2e® fuel cell system is a generator that converts gaseous hydrocarbon fuels (e.g. PNG/CNG) into electricity. The process is quieter, more efficient, and cleaner than conventional, engine-driven generators.

The h2e® fuel cell system is fuel flexible and can use a variety of fuels such as natural gas, liquefied petroleum gas, methane, and hydrogen. Energy from landfills, biomass, coal, wood, and agricultural waste can also be used to create the fuel. Because they function on such diverse input, fuel cells are a logical choice to transition from current combustion-based technologies to renewable energy sources.

The h2e® fuel cell system can be used for stationary applications to fulfil the heat and power demands of the Residential, Commercial, and Agricultural sectors.

Waste to Hydrogen:h2e collaborated with EPC providers for Waste to hydrogen production. The process creates value out of waste. It is a self-sustainable thermal conversion treatment of organic waste, including plastics, which breaks down the waste matter and extracts the hydrogen they contain.
tags: Cleantech / Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Renewable Energy / Sustainability

name: Polarium
one_liner: Provides lithium-ion energy storage solutions for commercial, industrial, and telecom applications.
description:
Energy storage absorbs and then releases power so it can be generated at one time and used at another. As the proportion of renewable, intermittent energy sources grow, energy storage is increasingly recognized as the missing link in the renewable energy system.

Polarium’s lithium battery energy storage solutions store energy safely, efficiently, and conveniently. Our solutions enable customers to reduce their energy costs and cut CO2-emissions.

Thanks to the embedded intelligence of lithium batteries, we turn uncertainty into predictability by always knowing the state of charge, (available capacity in real-time), and the state of health, (available life for optimal planning and usage).

Polarium provides backup, hybrid, standalone storage, solar and storage, EV (electric vehicle) charging, and microgrid solutions in the telecom, commercial, and industrial segments. Our energy storage solutions serve a wide variety of applications and operating environments.

name: Hy2gen AG
one_liner: Industrial-scale producer of green hydrogen and e-fuels including biomethanol and green ammonia for maritime, mobility, and industrial sectors
description:
Hy2gen is a producer of green hydrogen and hydrogen-based products such as biomethanol and green ammonia from carbon-free primary energy sources at an industrial scale for global distribution. Hy2gen also targets the production of renewable jet fuel and renewable natural gas (RNG). Hy2gen’s objective is to become the leader in the production of e-fuels worldwide with target markets are industries, maritime and mobility sectors. 

Hy2gen aims to provide products reaching the highest purity standards for the direct use in fuel cells (green hydrogen at 99.999%; biomethanol at 99.5% and green ammonia at 99.98%) and making them affordable as a sustainable solution to limit further changes in the earth’s climate. In addition to this, Hy2gen will provide green hydrogen to the increasing demand as chemical feedstock or direct fuel in engines.

Hy2gen has its main offices in Germany and three subsidiaries in Canada, France, and Norway.
investors: Caisse de Depot et Placement du Quebec
tags: Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Fuel Cells / Mobility / Renewable Energy / Transportation

name: HB11 Energy
one_liner: Develops laser hydrogen-boron fusion technology for clean, safe, and unlimited energy generation
description:
HB11’s aims to generate electricity using laser-ignited fusion of hydrogen and boron 11. This approach has been made viable by recent advances in laser technology called “Chirped Pulse Amplification” (CPA), the significance of which was recognized by the 2018 Nobel Prize in Physics. 

HB11 advantages:
The energy generation is safe – there are no radioactive waste problem and no risk of reactor melt-down. HB11 has much lower infrastructure costs and a smaller plant footprint compared to conventional nuclear or fossil fuel power plants. The HB11 concept does not require steam / gas turbines and electrical generators and there are no large-scale cooling requirements. The technology is suited for both grid integration and remote power generation and the compact size, low cost and lack of radioactive materials will open new market opportunities not possible with fission-based nuclear plants or DT fusion reactor concepts.
tags: Distributed Energy / Energy Optimization / Renewable Energy

name: Origen Hydrogen
one_liner: Origen enables the path to $1/kg green hydrogen by eliminating expensive rare-earth metal usage in electrolyzer hardware.
description:
Origen is leveraging a novel membrane chemistry to develop AEM electrolyzer hardware that is high-efficiency but without requiring rare-earth metals.

The value proposition is low-CAPEX which enables a path toward $1/kg green hydrogen. Origen is focused on the existing $160bn hydrogen market, which includes applications in the steel industry, ammonia (fertilizer), refineries, and green chemicals. Future markets include long-duration energy storage, replacing natural gas for heating, fuel cell trucks (e.g. Nikola), etc. 

Preparing to deploy first pilot in 4Q 2022.
tags: Advanced Manufacturing / Carbon Capture / Cleantech / Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Renewable Energy / Sustainability

name: NEL Hydrogen
one_liner: NEL Hydrogen is a world-leading supplier of hydrogen generation plants based on water electrolysis technology.
description:
Nel is a hydrogen company that delivers solutions to produce, store and distribute hydrogen from renewable energy. The company serves industries, energy and gas companies and its solutions cover the entire value chain from hydrogen production technologies to the manufacturing of hydrogen fueling stations.

Nel’s water electrolysers will help you reduce your operating costs, eliminate the need for delivered hydrogen, streamline your operations, and increase your bottom line. By producing hydrogen on-demand, they can also eliminate the need for stored hydrogen, saving valuable floor space and increasing plant safety. When connected to a source of renewable energy, these electrolysers will produce green hydrogen, reducing your plant’s carbon footprint and impact on the environment.

- Alkaline and PEM technologies.

- Safe, clean and cost effective.

- Consistent purity and pressure.

- Eliminate hydrogen delivery and storage.

- Scale to match any application.
investors: EASME - EU Executive Agency for SMEs

name: Neo-H2
one_liner: Creating hydrogen for clean energy solutions.
description: Advanced manufacturing technology, processes, or equipment for industrial production.
tags: Advanced Manufacturing / B2B / Biogas / Cleantech / Climate Change / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Fuel Cells / Fuel Efficiency / Maritime / Nano Coating / Nanotechnology / Oil & Gas / On-Demand

name: H2SITE
one_liner: Develops decentralized membrane reactors to produce renewable hydrogen locally for industrial and mobility applications
description:
H2SITE produces renewable H2 for small and medium comsumers for mobility and industry, solving the problem of inefficient H2 transport. We produce locally, using available resources: biogas, biomethane, ammonia, bioethanol... Cost-efficiency and feedstock flexibility are our main design criteria.

Within H2SITE we believe in the H2 economy, but we know it will only take place if we develop cost competitive solutions. That's why our driver is to produce renewable hydrogen at a fraction of the cost of the current technologies using patented membrane reactor technology.

Regarding market targets, it needs to be highlighted the industrial hydrogen production for small and medium users (below 1000 kg per day);  the mobility for maritime transport and mining sectors; decentralised power generation using coupled H2 generators with fuel cells; and hydrogen separation from blended gas streams (natural gas transport and distribution networks).
investors: Gestion De Capital Riesgo Del Pais Vasco

name: Green Hydrogen Systems
one_liner: Manufactures modular alkaline electrolyzers for scalable green hydrogen production
description:
At GHS, they have cracked the code to producing green hydrogen at any volume – from just a few hundred kW to multi-MW scale – at the lowest levelized cost of hydrogen (LCOH) available anywhere.

They have done it by combining our highly efficient alkaline electrolyzers, a modular, scalable approach to system design, and their on-site production philosophy – resulting in low-CAPEX electrolysis solutions that their customers are deploying today.
tags: Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Renewable Energy / Sustainability

name: Hydron Energy
one_liner: Manufactures PEM electrolyzer stacks and electrochemical cell technology for renewable hydrogen production
description:
Hydron Energy manufactures advanced electrochemical cell and membrane module technology for electrolysis processes based on polymer electrolyte membranes (PEM). Currently Hydron Energy offers a range of cutting edge PEM stack platforms that can be tailored to specific customer needs.

EL10:
The EL10 is multi versatile single cell electrolyser stack that is specifically used for R&D purposes.

EL25:
The EL25 is a high performance single/multi-cell cell electrolyser stack featuring an active area of 25 cm2 for materials R&D and small capacity electrolysis systems.The EL25 boasts high discharge pressure, a wide operating range and high conversion efficiency.

EL100:
The EL100 is Hydron’s reliable workhorse electrolyser platform. This electrolyser stack features a cell active area of 100 cm2. The stack can facilitate high current densities. The integrated liquid thermal management loop, various bores for sensor probes combined with the industrial architecture makes it an ideal solution for small/medium scale hydrogen production systems.

EL500:
Hydron’s largest capacity stack is the EL500, which features a cell active area of 500 cm2. This robust platform is the building block for intermediate range hydrogen production systems with a nominal capacity of 1 kg/hr.

name: OxEon Energy
one_liner: OxEon Energy is a company that provides energy products and services. It offers plasma reformers, Fischer-Tropsch reactors, solid oxide fuel cells,  and solid oxide electrolysis cells.
description: Solid oxide electrolysis for hydrogen and sustainable fuel production. Mars oxygen technology. 300+ patents.
tags: Advanced Manufacturing / Cleantech / Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Fuel Cells / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Renewable Energy / Sustainability

name: H2B2
one_liner: Develops and manufactures PEM electrolyzer systems for hydrogen production, offering end-to-end solutions from design to operation and maintenance
description:
H2B2 is a technology company that promotes, develops, finances, designs, integrates, builds, operates and maintains hydrogen production systems based on water electrolysis via PEM technology. The company operates in three main markets: industrial, energy storage and mobility.

Technology based, incorporated in 2016 and present in the United States and Spain. H2B2 provides innovation, design, engineering, manufacturing, integration, financing and O&M for modular hydrogen production systems, using water electrolysis. H2B2 develops small scale, medium scale and large scale electrolyzers. With regards to the large scale electrolyzers, H2B2’s largest-size of electrolyzers are able to supply hydrogen from 227.6 kg H2/day to 1,251 kg H2/day and these systems are integrated into a 40ft container. H2B2’s electrolyzers are certified and developed according to European or American (depending on customer geography) codes and standards. H2B2 supplies electrolyzers with CE marking and, if necessary, ETL stamp (priced separately), as well as required safety studies (HAZOP by default).

name: Hydros Power
one_liner: Hydros Power develops hydrogen cells and the recharging stations to refill empty cells for use in vehicles.
description: Automotive supply chain platform for parts logistics, manufacturing, or dealer network operations.
investors: No information about previous rounds or current investors is available.
tags: Battery / Battery Materials / Delivery / Last Mile Logistics / EV / Electronic Vehicles / Fuel Cells / Renewable Energy

name: Solesium Energy
one_liner: Develops carbon-negative energy systems using magnesium fuel cycles to produce green hydrogen and electricity
description:
Green. Carbon Negative. Reusable.

Solesium has been set up with the specific goal of kick starting the reusable energy revolution by commercialising a) the concept of using magnesium as fuel and b) regenerating it continuously.

The science behind our approach has already been confirmed. Solesium is aiming to be the first to combine the various elements together to ensure the reusable raw material cycle.

Our way of producing energy is not only carbon dioxide free, but in fact it consumes carbon dioxide during its process.

name: 1s1 Energy
one_liner: Develops low-cost proton exchange membrane technology to enable affordable green hydrogen production
description: 1s1 Energy is a Portugal and US-based company developing key technology that improves the economics of water electrolysis to eliminate the 300% to 400% 'green premium' that hampers the adoption of green hydrogen. A core element of the technology is a novel proton exchange membrane (PEM) that provides superior performance and durability at a fraction of the cost, enabling a Levelized Cost of Hydrogen production (LCOH) below $1.5/kg. 1s1 Energy will be producing PEMs, water electrolysis stacks, and 1 MW water electrolysis modules. The 1 MW modules will be integrated into a 2-10 MW distributed operation or combined to realize centralized plants with a capacity exceeding 100 MW. 1s1 scale-up plan includes working with large plant integrators that use third-party electrolysis stacks and modules to implement hydrogen fueling stations and large plants by first engaging in testing collaborations to develop these products to meet their specifications.
tags: Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Renewable Energy / Sustainability

name: Alchemr
one_liner: Alchemr is tackling the 30% of GHG emissions from hard-to-abate sectors through low-cost green hydrogen.
description:
Alchemr is a climate technology company addressing one of the most critical gaps in the global energy transition: decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors where direct electrification alone is insufficient. While renewable power can eliminate a significant portion of emissions, roughly 30% of global greenhouse gases remain tied to industries such as heavy transport, chemicals, and industrial heat. Alchemr targets this challenge by making low-cost green hydrogen commercially viable, positioning it as a scalable solution for industrial decarbonization and global energy equity.

At the core of Alchemr’s approach is the development of the world’s largest water electrolyzer based on anion exchange membrane (AEM) technology. Unlike conventional electrolyzers that rely on expensive rare metals and rigid operating conditions, Alchemr’s system is designed to operate efficiently with variable renewable power sources. This enables dramatically lower production costs and greater flexibility, removing two of the primary barriers that have historically made green hydrogen cost-prohibitive or operationally constrained.

In the near term, Alchemr delivers hydrogen-as-a-service through hydrogen refueling stations serving the heavy-duty transportation sector. This model allows fleets to adopt zero-emission fuel without upfront infrastructure investment. As the company scales electrolyzer manufacturing to gigawatt levels, Alchemr expands into equipment leasing for industrial customers in sectors such as green ammonia and methanol production, supporting large-scale decarbonization of chemical supply chains.

By combining renewable power, water, and cost-effective electrolysis, Alchemr is creating a profitable pathway to produce green hydrogen at industrial scale. The company’s solutions have the potential to reduce emissions by gigatons while generating substantial long-term revenue. In doing so, Alchemr aims not only to shift the economics of hydrogen, but to alter the trajectory of climate-driven global inequality by enabling cleaner, more accessible energy systems worldwide.
tags: Advanced Materials / Distributed Energy / Electrification / Renewable Energy / Sustainability

name: P2X Solutions
one_liner: P2X Solutions is a producer of green hydrogen and a pioneer in power-to-x technology.
description: P2X Solutions is a Finnish pioneer in green hydrogen and Power-to-X technology. They are building a 20 MW electrolyzer plant, which will start producing green hydrogen using electricity produced by wind, solar, biomass or hydropower and will be operational in 2024.  In addition to the plant to be implemented in Harjavalta, P2X Solutions aims to promote the Finnish hydrogen economy with several production plants and possibly also replicate projects abroad.
tags: Advanced Manufacturing / Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Renewable Energy

name: Gen2 Energy
one_liner: Gen2 Energy creates, runs, and manages a green hydrogen supply chain.
description:
Gen2 Energy is dedicated to developing, building, controlling and operating an integrated value chain for green hydrogen within 2024. The company aims to establish production capacity at large-scale for green hydrogen based on 100 percent renewable energy, and to operate an intelligent logistics network.

They are setting up their main distribution of green hydrogen in compressed form at 350 bar and are currently evaluating various compressions, and liquified hydrogen.  

At the moment, Gen2 Energy is working with the following production projects in Norway:
Suldal in Ryfylke region: Development of the site will be done in phases, initially with a production capacity of 10 MW, operational by 2023/24. Ultimately, the plan is to build a 300 MW production facility.

Mosjøen in Helgeland region: Gen2 Energy plans to build two production facilities for large scale production and shipping of green hydrogen, one in the Nesbruket area and one in the Holandsvika area. Both areas are now prepared for hydrogen production. This project will begin 2024. 

Meråker in Trøndelag region: Meraker Hydrogen facility has has a planned production capacity of 20 MW. The facility will be operational from 2024.
tags: Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Renewable Energy / Sustainability

name: sHYp
one_liner: sHYp has developed a membraneless electrolyser to produce hydrogen and other carbon-negative products from seawater or brine.
description:
sHYp has developed a membraneless electrolyser technology that uses sea water to produce green hydrogen and chemicals, including aqueous and solid hydroxides, acids, and other products for the building materials, carbon capture, and commodities market.

sHYp's technology introduces the first ever electrolyser to use sea water to produce hydrogen without the need for desalination or purification, and producing no toxic waste. This reducing significantly overhead costs of each project, while the co-production of hydroxide and other commodities creates multiple revenue streams — further reducing the 'green premium' of hydrogen produced.

sHYp's technology can be located in ports, on board vessels and FPSOs and offshore to harness surplus electricity generated by wind, wave and solar.
tags: Carbon Capture / Distributed Energy / Renewable Energy

name: Carbicrete
one_liner: Develops carbon-negative concrete technology that replaces cement with steel slag and sequesters CO2 during manufacturing
description:
Carbicrete is a Montreal-based cleantech company that develops innovative, low-cost building solutions that contribute to the reduction of greenhouse gas emissions. We offer concrete manufacturers the process, materials and support to make lower-cost, higher-quality precast concrete in a way that reduces their carbon footprint.

In our concrete, cement is replaced with steel slag—a by-product of the steel-making process that is often placed into landfills. Using a process called carbonation activation, CO2 is injected into the wet concrete to give it its strength. As a result, any product made using Carbicrete technology permanently sequesters CO2, while turning industrial waste (steel slag) into a high-quality construction product.

Concrete made using this process meets the same specifications as cement-based concrete, has lower material costs, and possesses better mechanical and durability properties.
investors: Ministère de l’Énergie et des Ressources Naturelles
tags: Advanced Materials / Building Materials / Construction / Construction Management / Construction Safety

name: Azolla
one_liner: Today, industrial manufacturing generates billions of tons of CO2 annually. Azolla’s biomanufacturing platform will empower industries to transform this CO2 into biomaterial that can be used to make everyday products, starting with textiles.
description: Advanced manufacturing technology, processes, or equipment for industrial production.
investors: The founding team has been bootstrapping the innitial discovery stage. We are looking for seed investors right now.

name: Prometheus Fuels
one_liner: Removes CO2 from the air and converts it into zero-net-carbon gasoline and jet fuel that competes with fossil fuels
description:
The Prometheus Titan Fuel Forge pulls CO2 and water from the air and combines them with electricity from solar and wind power to make zero net carbon fuels. In a year, a single Tital Fuel Forge can turn 900 tons of atmospheric CO2 into 100,000 gallons of gasoline, diesel, or jet fuel. These fuels are molecularly identical to those we put into cars and planes. 

• In stage 1, CO2 is removed from the air. Industrial fans suck air in and blow it across a waterfall that absorbs CO2 and additional water. 
• In stage 2, the salvaged CO2 encounters electricity in an electrochemical stack called the Faraday Reactor. The electricity 'charges' or 'energizes' the carbon with hydrogen molecules from the water to create long-chain alcohols. During this step, oxygen is released, making the forge a kind of mechanical forest. In one year, a single Titan Fuel Forge can emit 1,270 metric tocs of O2 into the atmosphere or roughly the same amount of oxygen emitted by 127 acres of forest.
• In step 3, the alcohols are harvested using a type of membrane called the Maxwell Core. Its 
pores are carbon nanotubes, which allow alcohols through while rejecting water.
• In stage 4, a catalyst step combines the alcohols and recovers water. This step can be customized to produce gasoline, diesel, or jet fuel.
tags: Air / Carbon Capture / Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Renewable Energy / Solar / Wind

name: Carbon Engineering
one_liner: Working to restore our climate with technology that removes carbon dioxide from the air at megaton-scale.
description: Founded in 2009, CE is a Canadian-based clean energy company leading the commercialization of groundbreaking technology that captures CO₂ directly from the atmosphere, and synthesizes it into clean, affordable transportation fuels. From a pilot plant in Squamish, B.C., CE has been removing CO₂ from the atmosphere since 2015 and converting it into fuels since December 2017.
tags: Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Renewable Energy / Sustainability

name: LanzaTech
one_liner: A carbon capture and recycling and repurposing technology.
description: Manufacturing operations platform within supply chain ecosystem - production, quality, or supplier management.

name: Energy Dome
one_liner: Develops grid-scale, long-duration energy storage systems using CO2-based thermodynamic technology
description:
EnergyDome is a company with a revolutionary technology for large scale and long duration energy storage. Energy Dome's proprietary technology is based on a thermodynamic process which uses CO2 to store electricity cost effectively with unprecedented round trip efficiencies. The simplicity of the process as well as the use of readily available off the shelf equipment opens the way for EnergyDome to quickly become an important player of the energy storage market. 

Energy Dome's technology allows to store large quantities of energy for long periods of time anywhere without any site dependency and deliver all the typical services of utility scale storage systems, including grid regulation services, maximizing investment returns for customers.

name: Carbix
one_liner: Carbix transforms atmospheric carbon dioxide into carbonates to build industrial products.
description: Carbix transforms atmospheric carbon dioxide into carbonates to build industrial products. Its novel bioreactor takes enriched CO2 from power and cement plants and in just hours converts it into cement and aggregates for the $900 billion construction industry, and home and yard goods for consumers looking to support the environment with their purchasing power. Carbix can sell state and federal carbon credits as a result of their impact on industrial emissions.
tags: Carbon Capture / Construction / Construction Safety / Sustainability

name: Dioxycle
one_liner: Converts industrial CO2 emissions into sustainable chemicals and fuels using electrochemical technology
description:
Dioxycle develops this idea to produce systems that turn industrial carbon dioxide into chemicals and fuels for a greener society. CO2 electrolysis offers a route to free us from crude oil dependence.

For years, chemicals and fuels have been produced by refining crude oil, which when burned eventually releases CO2. The more oil extracted, the more CO2 that accumulates in our atmosphere.

Dioxycle provides the electrochemical blocks capable of converting CO2 back into useful feedstocks, called e-fuels and e-chemicals. Their vision is a new, cleaner world where CO2 feeds our society, instead of threatening our climate.

Their team has been working on key technologies for electrochemical carbon dioxide conversion for over 5 years in the University of Cambridge (UK), Collège de France (France) and Stanford (US) before founding Dioxycle. By allying fundamental understanding to systematic scale-up, Dioxcycle believes they are beginning to unlock the full potential of this field.

name: Noya
one_liner: Noya is reversing climate change and pulling CO2 out of the sky by retrofitting existing pieces of equipment to capture carbon.
description:
Noya's mission is to accelerate the transition of industry to a sustainable existence. Every day, Noya is making progress towards this mission with a CO2 capture process that utilizes existing industrial equipment to pull CO2 out of the air. Noya takes the CO2 pulled from the atmosphere and re-packages it for sale to industrial CO2 consumers.

To achieve a future free of excess emissions, Noya is partnering with industrial equipment owners to deploy this carbon capture process onto their equipment. As the world's only distributed direct air capture company, Noya will lead the reversal of climate change and the development of the technology that will ensure a long, fruitful existence for humans on Earth.

one_liner: Turns captured CO2 into rock for permanent carbon storage
description:
44.01 turns CO₂ into rock, contributing to lower concentrations of CO₂ in the atmosphere. 44.01 then offers a permanent CO2 storage solution using the natural power of mineralization into peridotite.

To do this, 44.01 gathers the CO2 and partners with companies that use Direct Air Capture or other processes to remove carbon. Then, they partner with organizations that show legitimate interest in decarbonizing their processes (i.e., they are committed to reduce emissions and store the CO₂ that is connected to unavoidable emissions).

We work with world leading direct air capture technology partners. Their machines capture CO2 directly from the atmosphere. We then take their captured CO2 and mineralise it underground using our patented process.

name: Furno Materials
one_liner: Develops modular, carbon-neutral cement plants using oxyfuel combustion technology
description:
Unlike the large, fixed, capital-intensive plants of today, Furno is leveraging oxyfuel combustion and a novel design to develop plants that are more adaptable and energy-efficient, less capital-intensive and enable the production of zero-emission ordinary portland cement.

Furno has joined the Breakthrough Energy Fellowship in October 2021.

name: Twelve
one_liner: Twelve is a new kind of chemical company built for the climate era. They make essential products from air, not oil
description: Manufacturing operations platform within supply chain ecosystem - production, quality, or supplier management.
tags: Chemicals / Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Fuel Cells / Fuel Efficiency / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Renewable Energy

name: 75F
one_liner: IoT-native building automation platform for predictive HVAC control and energy management
description: 75F develops and deploys an Internet of Things (IoT) based Building Management System (BMS) targeted at commercial and institutional properties. Its solution stack includes wireless environmental and occupancy sensors, equipment controllers (Smart Nodes, Smart VAV, thermostats like MyStat and HyperStat), central control units (CCU), and cloud-hosted analytics platforms. These hardware components collect real-time data on temperature, humidity, CO₂, occupancy, and other parameters, which are transmitted over wireless mesh networks to gateways and then to cloud services for processing. Machine learning and proprietary control algorithms generate predictive HVAC zone control, dynamic airflow balancing, demand response, and indoor air quality management to optimize comfort, reduce energy consumption, and improve operational efficiency. The architecture supports remote system commissioning, over-the-air updates, secure cloud-based telemetry, and integration with existing HVAC infrastructure without extensive wiring or custom programming. 75F’s platform also includes portfolio-level visualization and control via web/mobile applications for facilities managers across multiple sites.
investors: Travis Connors | Cleantech Open | Cleantech Open
Seth London | Free Electrons | Google for Startups | Southern Minnesota Initiative Foundation | Stephen Case | Wells Fargo Innovation Incubator
tags: Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Internet of Things / IoT / Renewable Energy / Smart Buildings / Smart Home / Sustainability

name: Hearth Labs
one_liner: Develops smart sensors to measure thermal radiation and optimize comfort in buildings
description: In a normal building, half of a person's thermal comfort is determined by convection, air temprature and humidity while the other half is determined by thermal radiation. Hearth labs can calculate the exact heat transfer between a person and the environment, allowing them to accurately determine the thermal comfort. All the data comes from their product, Q Sensor. The smart sensor uses thermal radiation measurements and LiDAR technology to scan spaces. Combining these datasets with air temperature and humidity measures allows the sensor to fully measure thermal comfort. It's tripod mounted and used in a dianostic capacity to quantify heat transfer in the build environment. Their system can generate maps of the spatial distribution of comforts which shows where people are too hot or too cold.
tags: Architecture / BIM / Building Information Modeling / Building Analysis / Building Maintenance / Climate Change / Construction / Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Hardware / Renewable Energy / Sensors / Smart Buildings / Smart Home / Sustainability

name: Harvest Thermal
one_liner: Develops smart thermal storage and control systems for residential heat pumps to reduce emissions and energy costs
description:
Buildings produce over 20% of CO2 emissions in the US, and utilities still burn fossil fuels. Harvest Thermal provides disruptive heating, hot water and cooling for homes, uniquely delivering emissions reductions of 90%, while lowering bills and supporting the penetration of renewables on the grid. 

Harvest's proprietary controller manages a high efficiency heat pump and a hot water tank. With built-in thermal storage by default, it decouples the time of purchase of electricity from the time the energy is consumed, minimizing costs and emissions.

Harvest's systems have been endorsed by professionals in the energy sector, with a total of $1.45M from the California Energy Commission, National Science Foundation, and local utility PCE. Policies supporting building electrification are growing, with cities, utilities and states taking strong action to reduce emissions from homes. Harvest's unique capability positions it to capture this market, saving money and slashing emissions.

name: Thermoterra
one_liner: ThermoTerra captures a new source of renewable energy - Humidity Power - to heat and cool buildings, reducing energy consumption by up to 50%.
description: Technology company classified under manufacturing / Construction Technology taxonomy.

name: Power IQ
one_liner: Develops smart grid-aware appliances to optimize energy consumption and increase renewable energy usage
description: Power IQ is creating a new breed of Smart Grid aware appliances to make a revolution in the global electric power market. Power IQ is creating a new breed of green appliances, we help increasing renewable energy usage and saving our planet.

name: RepAir Carbon
one_liner: RepAir captures CO₂ from the air at Gigaton scale, with an ultra-low-cost solution.
description:
RepAir's Direct Air Capture Solution is inspired by battery and fuel cell principles. Its innovative electrochemical device utilizes electricity to separate CO₂ from the air. Their modular and scalable technology can reach Gigaton scale.

The system streams atmospheric air (~400ppm CO₂) into the electrochemical device, and applies electricity to separate CO₂ through a unique and selective membrane, cleaning the air that goes back to the atmosphere. The captured CO₂ streams out for utilization or storage.

RepAir's solution is net-negative, requires less energy, can be located at the point of CO₂ utilization, is modular and easily scalable, and requires no heat or water.

name: Charm Industrial
one_liner: Biomass gasification to hydrogen.
description: Manufacturing operations platform within supply chain ecosystem - production, quality, or supplier management.

name: Carbon Reform
one_liner: Recovering and recycling decentralized carbon emissions from commercial buildings
description:
Carbon Reform is a Delaware-based company focused on modular carbon capture for indoor air. Ventilation is an enormous financial burden on commercial building owners and operators, especially as organizations like the CDC encourage buildings to be over-ventilated to maintain good indoor air quality. 

State of the art variable air ventilation systems track building occupancy, correlating with CO2 and pathogen buildup, and increase ventilation during the peak hours of the day when electricity prices are highest. 

At Carbon Reform, we have created a smart appliance which will capture CO2, with drop-in filters for other harmful pollutants like VOCs, human bioeffluents, and viruses, and allow building owners to decrease their ventilation rates and save energy. The device captures and sequesters the CO2, allowing our customers to take advantage of carbon credits and work towards their sustainability goals.
tags: Building Analysis / Building Maintenance / Carbon Capture / Climate Change / Smart Buildings / Smart Home

name: Mission Zero
one_liner: Mission Zero electrochemically removes carbon dioxide from the air and concentrates it for a variety of sequestration pathways.
description:
Mission Zero is closing the carbon cycle gap by providing a new electrochemical DAC technology. 

Mission Zero's process captures CO2 continuously from air with a solvent, in the same way power plants cool wastewater. The CO2 in this solvent is then processed through scaled electrochemical purification technologies to capture a purified form of CO2.

Mission Zero's technology has been made possible through cost-effective electrochemistry, the growth of clean and renewable electricity, and the current technologies built to address water stress across the globe.

name: Global Thermostat
one_liner: Develops direct air capture technology to remove CO2 from ambient air for commercial and industrial applications
description: GT focuses on low-cost carbon negative technology that captures CO2 directly from Air and sells it  profitably for food and beverages for water desalination bio fertilizers to  produce synthetic gasoline and industrial gases. The GT methodology earned 32 patents in the US and in 147 nations, and received many awards, including world’s Top Ten Most Innovative Company (Fast Company Magazine).

name: CleanO2 Carbon Capture Technologies
one_liner: The world's first microscale carbon capture and heat recovery system.
description: CleanO2 Carbon Capture Technologies has developed a unique application, which directly removes approximately 5 metric tonnes of greenhouse gas emissions per year per unit, produces a revenue-generating by-product and contributes up to a 20% cost reduction in pre-heat water heating through a 80% efficient heat recovery technology.
tags: Advanced Manufacturing / B2B / B2B2C / B2C / Carbon Capture / Cleantech / Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Hardware / Renewable Energy / Software / Software as a Service / SaaS / Sustainability

name: CarbonOrO
one_liner: Upgrades landfill and biogas to renewable biomethane using low-energy amine-based carbon capture technology
description:
CarbonOrO uses a special amine solution to capture CO2 from raw gas. The installation consists of an absorber and a desorber. When raw landfill / mine gas enters the absorber, CO2 bonds to the amine solution and treated methane leaves the absorber at the top. The amine solution is then pumped to the desorber where CO2 is removed by heating before the amine solution is recirculated to the absorber to repeat the process.

The majority of the world’s municipal solid waste ends up in landfills. Within those landfills anaerobic degradation, a naturally occurring process, generates landfill gas which mainly consists of methane and carbon dioxide. Methane is a highly potent greenhouse gas that can be captured and used in a variety of applications. When methane is collected from landfills and used it not only prevents the gas from escaping into the atmosphere, but also displaces the carbon dioxide emissions from the fossil fuels that would otherwise have been used. The methane coming out of your landfill can be converted into the renewable equivalent (biomethane) of natural gas- which will turn your landfill into a renewable energy source and improve your ‘green’ image.
investors: EASME - EU Executive Agency for SMEs

name: Holy Grail
one_liner: Holy Grail uses electrons to capture carbon dioxide from the atmosphere.
description:
Holy Grail is prototyping a direct air carbon capture device that is modular and small — a departure from the dozens of projects in the U.S. and abroad that aim to capture CO2 from large, centralized emitters, like power plants or industrial facilities.

Cost-effectively separating CO2 from the air is an extremely difficult problem to solve. Holy Grail is taking a fundamentally different technical approach to carbon capture by using a modular carbon capture solution that uses electricity to control a chemical reaction that binds to the CO2. Holy Grail’s devices are not dependent on scale to achieve cost reductions, and they will be modular, so they can be stacked or configured depending on a customer’s requirements.

These carbon scrubbers will focus on raw capture of CO2 rather than conversion.

name: Capture
one_liner: A mobile app enabling individuals to track, reduce and remove CO2 emissions from everyday life
description:
*In process of acquisition*

Our app helps the climate-conscious individual to track, reduce and remove CO2 emissions from everyday life. Right now, our algorithms can automatically track emissions from daily mobility choices, but we are fast-expanding tracking into additional categories (such as food consumption and home energy usage).

Since launching our MVP on Google Play and the App Store in early January, Capture has been responding to inbound enquiries from businesses wanting to reduce carbon emissions from corporate travel, and are now developing ‘Capture for Teams’, a B2B Sustainability Benefit solution with a fast-growing waitlist including multinationals, global luxury brands and start-ups around the world.

We are responding to the unstoppable wave of awareness around climate change and the unprecedented sense of urgency for individuals and businesses to take action. Defining a new category, our vision is to build tech that unleashes climate warriors around the world.

10,000 organic downloads, signing our first corporate client, closing our angel round

Deck(s): https://api.typeform.com/responses/files/d418e80e2d65eb1c737b101eac002cf78d3f324a4dfb6a08498b028db85598dd/Capture_Pitch_Deck.pdf
Video(s): https://youtu.be/mSvCQbZjeVw

name: Carbonova
one_liner: Develops carbon nanofibers from greenhouse gases using proprietary processes and catalysts
description:
Carbonova is a technology company developing a solution to produce high-volume superior carbon nanofibers (Carbonova fiber) for use in massive market applications. Carbonova technology utilizes huge amounts of greenhouse gas feedstocks annually and turns them into a sustainable and valuable solid commodity. The company owns the proprietary process and catalysts formulation. With headquarters in Alberta, Carbonova is on track to building the first large scale commercial carbon nanofibers unit in Canada.

Carbon nanofiber is a material with extraordinary mechanical, thermal, electrical, and chemical properties. It has applications in a wide variety of industries including transportation vehicles, concrete, electronic devices, textiles, ink, coatings, lubricants, tires, and agriculture. We produce carbon nanofiber from a novel process that utilises carbon dioxide and methane. Our feedstocks have low cost and high global warming impacts, and we transform them into a material that is valuable and in demand.

name: CRA-Tubular
one_liner: Develops carbon fiber spoolable tubing for oil, gas and geothermal applications
description:
CRA-Tubulars is a newly established Company backed by many years of excellence and innovation. The Company operates in the oil and gas space and seeks to help oil companies to solve the issue of well-integrity, associated cost and subsequent loss of oil production. The Company is in the final stage of designing a Carbon Fiber Spoolable Tubing (or ‘CFST’) that aims to disrupt the deployment of metallic tubulars in upstream oil and gas.

CFST is the first non-metallic alternative specifically tailored to the drilling industry that is dominated by tubulars based on metallic variants. The product development is expected to yield several patents on the design, material usage, production process, and material chemistry. Patent research has shown that there are currently no patents that prevent the Company from entering the market.

CRA-Tubulars is an initiative by Emile Burnaby Lautier & cooperates with Voith Composites Se & Co. KG.
Voith sets standards in the markets for energy, oil & gas
investors: Angle Investors and Regional Investment Fund

name: Mineral Carbonation International
one_liner: Transforms industrial CO2 emissions into solid materials for low-carbon building and construction products
description:
Mineral Carbonation International (MCi) is an Australian clean technology company transforming CO2 emissions into valuable materials that provide profitable decarbonisation pathways for the mining, manufacturing and energy sectors.

MCi uses carbon engineering processes to transform captured CO2 emissions from most industrial sources into solid materials that can be used to manufacture a range of low carbon building and construction products. We use CO2 as a resource and create profitable pathways for many industries to reduce their emissions.

In 2020, following a successful 7-year period of research funded by Australian Governments and industry partners, we began our commercialisation process. Our expertise and solid techno-economic models allow us now to work with a range of national and international customers and partners as we scale our technology.

name: CarbonQuest
one_liner: Develops onsite carbon capture systems for commercial buildings to reduce CO2 emissions from natural gas
description:
CarbonQuest has developed a patent pending process to perform carbon capture on site in the built environment which will reduce CO2 from natural gas emissions. We use a four step process to capture carbon: 

Flue Gas Extraction
Separation and Purification
Liquefaction
Storage and Removal

Our system is economical with a low CAPEX and OPEX for continuous unattended operations. It is safe and modular, having no flammability and being sized to the building and thermal load. The results are measurable through full system monitoring and control of all processes including volume and purity of captured CO2.

CarbonQuest is installing onsite carbon capture at properties in New York City with a well-respected property developer. The projects is expected to cut 40-70% of CO2 emissions from natural gas usage. The goals of these project includes 1) provide carbon reductions economically now 2) reduce exposure to Local Law 97 3) Repurposing the SustainableCO2 into local beneficial utilization applications closing the local circular carbon economy.

name: CarbonCure Technologies
one_liner: CarbonCure Technologies creates, develops and licenses solutions that consume waste CO2 to make better concrete.
description: Technology company classified under manufacturing / Construction Technology taxonomy.

name: Clean Carbon Technologies
one_liner: Builds reactors that convert industrial flare gases into carbon nanotubes and graphene
description: We build reactors that capture and convert emissions, especially flare gases from Industries into Carbon nanotubes and Graphene.
tags: B2B / B2B2C / B2C / Carbon Capture / Distributed Energy / Energy Efficiency / Energy Saving / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Infrastructure / Renewable Energy / Smart Infrastructure

name: Skytree
one_liner: Develops direct air capture technology to generate CO2 from ambient air for industrial and agricultural applications.
description:
Founded in 2014 and headquartered in Amsterdam, Skytree harnesses CO2 from ambient air through the power of direct air capture technology (DAC). By providing local, onsite CO2 generation across multiple markets including indoor farming, greenhouses as well as mineralization, Skytree enables a transition away from fossil fuel based industrial processes and avoids transport to where the CO2 is needed.  The company’s core technology captures CO2 from ambient air and stores it in pressurized buffer tanks where it can be accessed for multiple applications. The modular technology can be set up quickly at any location and scaled to meet the needs of businesses of all sizes. 

Born out of the European Space Agency (ESA), during the development of a CO2 scrubber, space scientists evaluated over 50 sorbents. Skytree founder, Max Beaumont, was a system engineer on this team and spun out the technology through the ESA incubator program 'ESA-BIC'. With a decade of research & development, the company has since registered 17 patents, in 5 patent families across US, Europe and China and is ready to monetize the technology through multiple use cases.
tags: B2B / B2B2C / Carbon Capture / Cleantech / Energy Optimization / Energy Storage / Energy Trading / Grid Storage / Energy Grid / Hardware / Renewable Energy / Sustainability / Waste Reduction / Waste Management

name: NovoNutrients
one_liner: Transforms industrial CO2 emissions into protein-rich food and feed ingredients via microbial bioprocessing
description:
Our technology makes ingredients for purchase by manufacturers of food and feed. By upcycling industrial waste gases, notably carbon dioxide (CO2), this bioprocessing utilization of carbon capture forms the core of our platform. Our licensees build plants to make bulk proteins and/or additives.

Our primary product is a proprietary bioprocessing platform that transmutes industrial carbon dioxide emission waste gas into food and feed materials. Gas streams are dissolved into an aqueous solution, inside our patent-pending bioreactor. That grows up a designed community of microorganisms, which are harvested, sterilized, and dried. The results can be either a single-cell microbial protein flour or one of a number of food and feed additives. Among what sets us apart from our most direct competitors, is the efficacious microbial consortia approach. That allows the platform to achieve a high degree of efficiency, as well as variety, in producing bulk and niche products for food and agriculture.
investors: Jeremy and Hannelore Grantham Environmental Trust

description: Orchestra Sci enables selective CO2 separation, as well as outstanding CO2 uptake and selectivity with respect to other gases like: CH4,N2, O2, H2, CO in dynamic breakthrough conditions. Orchestra Sci allows for a complete retainment of dynamic CO2 uptake capacity in presence of gas mixture with different composition; working in in a wide CO2 concentration range (1% - 99%); working in mild conditions: ambient pressure and wide temperature range (RT - 80ºC); high yield (up to 99%) recovering of other gases with high purity (≥ 99.5 %); high working capacity (up to 99%): easy and fast regeneration with vacuum and or gas flow (Air, N2, CH4…); possibility of high purity CO2 recovering (≥ 99.5 %); as well as high stability: until now 7 month working without any sign of degradation.

Their step change technology significantly reduces the costs and environmental impacts over existing CO2 separation techniques. With prime focus on innovation, the startup aims to provide customised low cost, energy efficient solutions for carbon dioxide separation to their customers. Their globally spread teams strive to help large scale CO2 emitters in solving the challenges of continued usage of non-renewable fossil fuels, and reducing the environmental impact of high carbon dioxide emissions.

one_liner: Carbon America transforms the carbon capture and sequestration (CCS) industry by lowering costs and rapidly increasing deployment.
description: Carbon America transforms the carbon capture and sequestration (CCS) industry by lowering costs and rapidly increasing deployment. Carbon America is a carbon capture and sequestration developer. It is vertically integrated, nimble, and built to scale, with a multi-strategy approach to capture and sequester carbon dioxide faster, cheaper, and smarter than the competition. To achieve this, Carbon America has built a team combining world-class engineers, developers, and financiers into a vertically integrated CCS company.

name: DAC City
one_liner: Builds containerized direct air capture machines to extract atmospheric CO2 for greenhouse enrichment, fuel production, and concrete sequestration
description:
We have active projects in carbon dioxide enrichment and capture with field trial deployment scheduled for early 2020.
CO2 captured from our shipping container sized machine will be used for making methanol fuels and sequestering CO2 into concrete.
Our core technology is built on decades of deployment of highly porous carbon composite ceramic monoliths, utilizing a proprietary method of surface activation, and formation and control of pore geometry, enhancing the adsorption, desorption, working capacity, and energetics of carbon dioxide capture.

Our data science team is integrating machine learning into measurement and verification of carbon dioxide sorption, for assured “for value” transactions in emerging carbon market adoption of negative emission technology.

name: MechanicalTrees
one_liner: The world's first scalable energy efficient CO₂ Direct Air Capture solution.-
description:
The MechanicalTree™ is the most radical and significant breakthrough in carbon capture globally. It is both scalable (gigaton capable) and energy efficient - two key limitations of other approaches to Direct Air Capture. The MechanicalTree™ can be be deployed for capture of current and historical emissions.

Silicon Kingdom Holdings Ltd (SKH) and wholly-owned U.S. subsidiary Carbon Collect Inc. has been formed in partnership with Arizona State University, and with the collaboration of global energy and climate change leaders. The technology is based on the latest innovations of Dr Klaus Lackner and his team at the Center for Negative Carbon Emissions at ASU.

MechanicalTrees™ are a thousand times more efficient than natural trees at removing CO2 from the air. It offers a key solution for business and political leaders striving to meet net-zero and low carbon emission targets.

one_liner: Carbominer helps greenhouse growers to move to more efficient and sustainable horticulture with our microclimate monitoring system and CO2 captured from the open air.
description: Agricultural technology for farming operations, precision agriculture, or farm management software.
tags: Agtech / Agriculture / Industry 4.0 / Industrial IoT / Sustainability

name: Again
one_liner: Transforms industrial CO2 emissions into valuable chemicals using on-site carbon capture technology
description: SecondCircle offers its CO2-to-value-technology as a service, where the SecondCircle technology is installed on the CO2 emitter's production site. SecondCircle's carbon capture and utilization platform includes a proprietary reactor in which a microorganism with superior traits converts CO2 and hydrogen into new value. The technology enables storage of hydrogen generated from renewable energy at off-peak hours, also described as Power-to-X. CO2 is converted into the bulk chemical acetate. Acetate has a market size of more than $ 9B and has various applications in industry, where it is upgraded to plastic polymers, adhesives, and even human food. SecondCircle has revenue streams from recurring per-unit-payment for removal of CO2, as well as sales of the end-product. The company is currently in pilot phase and has attracted development partners from various industry segments.

name: CarbonBuilt
one_liner: CarbonBuilt enables concrete manufacturing to drive greenhouse gas reductions through the utilization of CO2 and other industrial wastes.
description: CarbonBuilt's Carbon XPrize-winning technology enables the production of ultra low-carbon concrete products, driving gigatonne-scale greenhouse gas reductions through the cost-effective mineralization of carbon dioxide. Our revolutionary approach reduces overall carbon dioxide emissions by 70-100%+, with no compromise on price, performance, or plant operations.

name: Northern Lights
one_liner: Northern Lights delivers carbon storage as a service.
description:
We are developing an open and flexible infrastructure to transport CO2 from capture sites by ship to a terminal in western Norway for intermediate storage, before being transported by pipeline for permanent storage in a reservoir 2,600 metres under the seabed.

Our transport and storage facilities will offer safe and permanent underground storage to industries from across Europe.

The project is the transport and storage component of Longship, the Norwegian Government’s full-scale carbon capture and storage project Northern Lights will be the first ever cross-border, open-source CO2 transport and storage infrastructure network. Phase one of the project will be completed mid-2024 with a capacity of up to 1.5 million tonnes of CO2 per year.

name: D-CRBN
one_liner: Develops plasma technology to convert CO2 into sustainable fuels, chemicals, and polymers
description:
D-crbn, a spin-off company from the research group plasmant, within the university of antwerp has developed a cutting edge technology that enables the conversion of co2.
This innovative process uses plasma to split co2 molecules into its original building blocks.
These building blocks can be re-used to create valuable products such as biofuels, chemicals, or polymers to ultimately achieve a circular and more sustainable economy.

name: Carbon Limit
one_liner: Develops carbon-negative cement that transforms concrete into a permanent carbon capture and storage solution.
description:
The concrete industry is one of the largest producers of carbon dioxide. Considering all stages of production, concrete is estimated to be responsible for around 8% of the world’s CO2 emissions.

Carbon Limit’s nature based technology quantifiably reduces the carbon footprint of the concrete manufacturing process and transforms the concrete into a permanent to help decarbonize the cement and concrete industries. The product is a carbon negative cement that is a simple drop in solution that doesn’t require any new equipment, processing, or CAPEX from the customer. The technology allows any cement and concrete company to offer a lower carbon footprint concrete product.
tags: B2B / Biotechnology / Carbon Capture / Cleantech / Climate Change / Construction / Nanotechnology / Smart Buildings / Smart Home / Smart City / Sustainability

description:
Kiverdi is fighting the climate crisis with technology that recycles carbon to address global issues like sustainable food production and the ocean’s plastic problem.

Kiverdi’s NASA-inspired technologies convert carbon dioxide into people and planet-friendly nutrients and bio-based materials. The company’s patent-protected commercial solutions are being used by some of the leading manufacturers to power a new era of sustainable production — all while tackling some of our most pressing global challenges.

The solutions include: 

Reverse Plastics – solving the plastics crisis with a circular technology that transforms plastic into biodegradable materials 
Revive Soil – increasing crop yield with organic nutrients that shift CO2 from the air to the soil
CO2 Aquafeed – meeting the demand of feed with an alternative sustainable protein source
Custom Cycle – reaching circular economy goals wit

description: CO2 Solutions is a carbontech leader in the field of enzyme-enabled carbon capture and has been actively working to  commercialize and improve the technology for stationary sources of carbon pollution. CO2 Solutions’ technology lowers the cost barrier to Carbon Capture, Utilization and Sequestration (CCUS), positioning it as a viable CO2 mitigation tool, as well as enabling industry to derive profitable new products from these emissions. CO2 Solutions has built an extensive patent portfolio covering the use of carbonic anhydrase, or analogues thereof, for the efficient post-combustion capture of carbon dioxide with low‐energy aqueous solvents.   The Corporation is completing its first commercial project, a 30-tonne per day capture unit in Saint-Felicien (QC) and leads the world's most comprehensive carbon capture and utilization project, demonstrating solutions to capture and utilize CO2 in various value-added applications while reducing greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions.

It'a an All-in-One Solution: Their Climate Impact Manager is your one-stop-shop for climate action: Planetly unites the entire carbon management process in one easy-to-use platform - keep track of your emissions and take immediate action.

Our tool automates the data collection process, delivers accurate results in line with reporting frameworks, and generates decarbonisation strategies. Companies can visualise results in a cohesive dashboard, and add custom modules for more complex and industry-specific Scope 3 use cases (i.e. financed emissions according to PCAF). We have developed an extensive database of emissions factors and averages across industries, geographies, and activities which empower our customers to achieve accurate emissions calculations even when they have trouble finding some of their data.
